name: Abay's lechon de cebu bar and grill
description:
Abay's Lechon de Cebu Bar and Grill is a Filipino restaurant in Mount Gravatt, Brisbane, specialising in authentic Cebuano cuisine. The restaurant is well known for its signature Cebu-style lechon belly, praised by diners for its crunchy skin and rich flavour. The menu features a wide selection of traditional favourites including ngohiong, balbacua, sinigang na bangus, humba, pansit canton, lumpia, sisig, daing, and halo-halo. Turon with vanilla ice cream is a popular dessert choice.

Customers appreciate the warm, family-friendly atmosphere and the attentive, friendly service from the staff. The venue has hosted wedding receptions, birthday celebrations, and other special gatherings, with many reviewers highlighting the team's flexibility and genuine care for their guests. Live music adds to the lively dining experience on select evenings.

Located at 1420 Logan Rd, the restaurant offers plenty of free parking options including underground parking. Diners describe the food as great value, with most spending between $20 and $60 per person. The Cebu-style flavours are frequently praised as authentic, with several customers noting the dishes remind them of eating in Cebu itself.

Please note that this business is currently temporarily closed.

name: Ang Hiyas
description:
Ang Hiyas is a Filipino formalwear boutique based in Holmview, Logan City, dedicated to elevating Filipino heritage through traditional clothing with a modern touch. Specialising in Barong Tagalog and Filipiniana, the shop offers custom-made tailoring, ready-to-wear garments, and rental options for weddings, cultural celebrations, graduations, and formal occasions.

Beyond formalwear, Ang Hiyas curates a range of Philippine-made artisan accessories, including handwoven bags and traditional footwear. Simple alterations are also available, making it a convenient one-stop destination for formal events. Whether you need a perfectly fitted barong for an oath-taking ceremony or an elegant filipiniana for a community gala, early booking is recommended to secure your preferred design and size.

Serving customers across Queensland and Australia, Ang Hiyas brings authentic Filipino craftsmanship to the diaspora community. The boutique has built a loyal following among Filipino-Australians looking for quality traditional attire that honours their cultural heritage while embracing contemporary style.

name: Arc Migration
description:
Arc Migration (also known as Arc Visa & Migration) is a professional immigration and citizenship services firm based in Woolloongabba, Brisbane. Led by lawyer and registered migration agent Ehsan Azadi alongside Fides Velarde, a dedicated Filipina migration specialist, the team has built a strong reputation within the Filipino-Australian community for their honest, compassionate, and highly responsive support.

Clients frequently praise the firm for simplifying complex visa processes and offering clear, step-by-step guidance. The team specialises in a wide range of services, including skilled work visas, partner visas, citizenship applications, visitor visas, and representation for Administrative Appeals Tribunal (AAT/ART) reviews or refusals. With their meticulous preparation and attention to detail, many clients have successfully secured their permanent residency and citizenship, often remarking on the team's ability to ease the stress of the migration journey.

Conveniently located at 80 Ipswich Road, Arc Migration is a trusted partner for individuals and families looking to build their future in Australia.

name: Ate Perla (Forex Cargo Agent)
description:
Ate Perla is a friendly and reliable local agent for Forex Cargo Australia, operating from Caboolture in Queensland. She specialises in supporting the local Filipino-Australian community by providing essential logistics and shipping services to the Philippines.

As an authorised empty box outlet, Ate Perla coordinates the distribution and collection of balikbayan boxes. Locals can easily pick up empty boxes of various sizes and purchase packing supplies to prepare their care packages for loved ones. Once packed, she assists in coordinating the collection and shipment process, ensuring that each box is handled safely and efficiently by Forex Cargo's extensive delivery network.

Conveniently located at 34 George Street in Caboolture, this outlet makes sending packages straightforward and stress-free for residents in North Brisbane. Ate Perla's personalised service and helpful guidance ensure that community members feel supported when sending support back home. Whether you need box collection details, packing advice, or general shipping timelines, her local service is a valuable connection for the Filipino community in the region.

name: Ate Perla - Forex Cargo Agent
description:
Ate Perla is an authorised outlet agent for Forex Cargo Australia, operating from Caboolture in the Moreton Bay region of Queensland. She serves as a vital connection for the local Filipino-Australian community, offering reliable cargo services to send packages home to families in the Philippines. Sending a balikbayan box is a cherished tradition, and Ate Perla helps make this process smooth and stress-free.

As an official empty box outlet, she provides authentic Forex balikbayan boxes and packing supplies directly to local residents. Customers can choose between sea freight for large, heavier shipments and air freight for more time-sensitive packages. Both options include secure door-to-door delivery to destinations across the Philippine archipelago.

Located at 34 George Street in Caboolture, this convenient outlet assists families throughout the northern Brisbane and Moreton Bay areas. Customers can reach Ate Perla directly on 07 5495 8041 to purchase empty boxes, check upcoming shipping departure dates, or ask questions about import regulations and customs declarations. Her friendly service ensures every care package is properly prepared for its journey overseas.

name: Auntie Grace Filipino Store
description:
Owned and operated by Grace Moyle (also known as Grace Cajulo), Auntie Grace Filipino Store has recently relocated to Raceview, continuing to bring a warm slice of the Philippines to the Ipswich community. Local shoppers highly value the friendly and helpful customer service provided by Auntie Grace herself.

The store specialises in a diverse selection of authentic Filipino groceries, including classic Pinoy snacks, condiments, and frozen goods, making it easy to find ingredients for favourite dishes. In addition to pantry staples, the shop offers a curated range of everyday fashion, popular clothing brands, and quality gold jewellery.

Many customers describe the welcoming shop as a little piece of home. While the business has moved to its new location on Warner Street in Raceview, the owner is actively working to update online maps. Visitors are encouraged to contact the store directly to confirm the address and enjoy a warm, community-focused shopping experience.

name: Auntie Grace Filipino Store in Ipswich
description:
Auntie Grace Filipino Store is a welcoming local grocery shop located in Raceview, Ipswich. Owned and operated by Grace Moyle, who previously managed a successful grocery business in the Philippines, this family-run store brings a comforting taste of home to the local Filipino community.

The shop specialises in a diverse selection of authentic Pinoy groceries, popular snacks, and frozen goods. Customers can easily find essential ingredients to prepare their favourite traditional dishes. Beyond the extensive grocery aisles, the store also offers quality gold jewellery and everyday fashion items.

Visitors frequently highlight the friendly and helpful customer service provided by the owners, describing the atmosphere as warm and inviting. Prices are highly affordable, making it a convenient one-stop destination for daily essentials. Following a recent relocation to Raceview, the store continues to build strong relationships with its shoppers, offering both in-store browsing and online ordering with local pickup options. Whether you are searching for specialty ingredients or everyday clothing, this store provides a friendly and authentic piece of the Philippines in Queensland.

name: Auspac Visa
description:
Auspac Visa is a Brisbane-based migration agency specialising in Australian immigration law and visa processing services. Established in 2006, the agency assists the Filipino community with various visa pathways, including student, skilled migrant, partner, and family visas. Teresa Cardona, a Filipino migrant and admitted lawyer for the Supreme Court of Queensland, leads the team.

Clients highly recommend the agency for its clear, direct, and professional guidance. Many appreciate the team's patience and dedication, especially when navigating complex application processes. Reviewers often mention that the consultations are very straightforward, helping them understand their visa options and make informed decisions with confidence. The team works hard to make the transition from student visas to permanent residency as smooth and stress-free as possible.

Located in the heart of Brisbane City, the agency provides legal representation and advice tailored to each client's unique migration journey. Their office is situated on Eagle Street, making it easily accessible for local clients seeking trusted support to achieve their Australian goals.

name: Australian Filipino Augustinian Solidarity
description:
The Australian Filipino Augustinian Solidarity (AFAS) is a dedicated community ministry program operating from Villanova College in Coorparoo, Brisbane. Established in 1993 by passionate teachers following an Augustinian conference in Manila, this long-standing partnership fosters deep cross-cultural friendship and solidarity. It connects students, staff, and the broader Brisbane community with Augustinian communities in Cebu and Bacolod in the Philippines.

A central focus of the program is providing life-changing educational opportunities. AFAS actively raises funds to support scholarships for talented yet disadvantaged students in the Philippines, helping them access quality schooling. The group also assists with vital community infrastructure and outreach projects, including school resource development, renovations, and local livelihood initiatives.

Through regular immersion exchanges and friendship trips, participants gain valuable insights into Filipino culture and lifestyle. This active involvement nurtures global awareness and builds lasting bonds between the two nations. The program also engages with local community events in Brisbane, such as the Barrio Fiesta, to raise awareness and celebrate cultural heritage.

name: Australian Filipino Language and Culture Association, Inc.
description:
Based in the northern suburb of Caboolture in Queensland, the Australian-Filipino Language and Culture Association Incorporated is a dedicated non-profit community group. This organisation plays a vital role in keeping Filipino heritage alive for families and residents in the Moreton Bay region. It focuses on sharing the rich language, customs, and values of the Philippines with the local community.

The association provides structured lessons in Tagalog grammar and literature. These classes help younger generations and interested community members develop language literacy. Beyond formal language education, the group organises cultural activities that highlight Filipino traditions, music, and cuisine. These gatherings foster a strong sense of connection and offer educational support to newly arrived families.

By celebrating shared values and heritage, the association creates a welcoming space for cultural exchange and friendship. Whether you want to learn Tagalog or connect with others who share a love for Filipino culture, this local group offers a supportive and engaging environment. They meet at local community hubs, such as the Caboolture Neighbourhood Centre, to run their programs and host family-friendly events.

name: Averillaw Solicitors
description:
Averillaw Solicitors is a modern, fixed-fee online law practice specialising in providing clear and stress-free legal services to the Filipino-Australian community. Led by Mark Averilla, a dual-licensed lawyer in both the Philippines and Australia, the firm delivers reliable assistance across Queensland and beyond. Clients can comfortably discuss their matters in Tagalog or Bicol, ensuring all legal processes are easy to understand.

The practice specialises in conveyancing, immigration law, wills and estates, and Philippine property matters. Clients frequently praise Mark for his approachability, integrity, and clear communication. Home buyers appreciate his transparent fees and prompt support during complex property transactions. He is also highly regarded for his expert handling of visa applications, helping families successfully navigate partner and spouse visas, even in cases with a history of prior refusals. Furthermore, community members note his genuine care and empathy when resolving sensitive estate and family law matters.

Operating entirely online, Averillaw Solicitors makes high-quality legal support accessible and convenient. Whether you are purchasing your first home in Brisbane, applying for a visa, or managing property affairs in the Philippines, this firm offers a supportive and professional partnership.

name: B & E Auto and Daily Service
description:
B & E Auto and Daily Service is a family-owned workshop in Acacia Ridge, Brisbane, offering high-quality automotive service and repairs. Highly regarded by the local community, this workshop is run by Boyet, an experienced mechanic who has spent over twenty years honing his trade across the Philippines, the United Arab Emirates, and Australia.

Customers consistently praise the shop for its honest, reliable, and professional approach to car care. The skilled team is known for keeping vehicle owners updated throughout the day, providing clear explanations and photographic proof of any parts replaced. Whether it is a routine logbook service, vehicle diagnostics, or complex repairs like replacing brake pads, rotors, clutches, and tyres, the mechanics tackle every job with efficiency and fair pricing. Backpackers and local residents alike appreciate their accommodating nature and honest troubleshooting skills, which rival Euro car specialists in the area.

Conveniently located at Unit 5, 51 Overlord Place, the business is dedicated to vehicle safety and customer convenience, accepting modern payment plans like Afterpay, humm, and Zip. With a reputation for honest advice and friendly service, B & E Auto and Daily Service is a trusted choice for the local Brisbane community.

name: BRIGITTS BAKESHOP
description:
Brigitts Bakeshop is a specialised Filipino dessert shop and bakery located in the Pacific Centre at 223 Calam Road, Sunnybank Hills. Serving the local Brisbane community, this boutique cake shop is highly regarded for its handcrafted treats that are loaded with authentic flavours but balanced to ensure they are not overly sweet.

Customers rave about their signature ube creations, including a fluffy ube cake, rich ube flan, and a popular ube brownie burnt basque cheesecake topped with a macadamia coconut crumble. Other favourites include their pandan cake cups, chunky cookies, and refreshing specialty drinks like the ube latte and strawberry matcha.

Operating primarily as a takeaway venue, the bakery provides friendly and welcoming service to everyone who walks through the doors. The shop operates from Wednesday to Sunday, closing on Mondays and Tuesdays. Because their freshly prepared cakes and confections frequently sell out, placing pre-orders online through their website is highly recommended to secure your favourite desserts.

name: Barkada Hub
description:
Barkada Hub is a welcoming social group based in Brisbane, Queensland, designed to bring Filipinos and their friends together. The community focuses on building close friendships, sharing stories, and celebrating Filipino culture. In the Filipino language, the term 'barkada' refers to a close-knit group of friends, which perfectly reflects the warm and supportive atmosphere of the group.

The group provides a great opportunity for locals to network, make new connections, and enjoy delicious food together. With a growing community of twenty-six members, they organise regular social gatherings and meetups across Brisbane. Past events include a lively Filipino Meet and Greet Happy Hour, hosted at the Netherworld bar in Fortitude Valley, where attendees gathered to share food, laugh, and socialise.

Whether you want to explore traditional Filipino customs, share experiences about living in Australia, or simply meet new people in a friendly environment, Barkada Hub offers a comfortable space for everyone. It is a fantastic network for anyone looking to connect with the local Filipino community in Brisbane and make lasting memories.

name: Barrio Fiesta Brisbane
description:
Barrio Fiesta Brisbane Inc is a registered not-for-profit community organisation in Brisbane. The association works to promote and celebrate the rich Filipino culture and heritage across Queensland. By collaborating with other multicultural communities, the group helps build bridges of mutual understanding.

The organisation is best known for hosting the annual Barrio Fiesta Brisbane festival at the Rocklea Showgrounds. This popular event celebrates Philippine Independence Day with vibrant cultural performances, traditional dances, live music, and authentic Filipino food stalls. It brings the local Filipino-Australian community together while welcoming the wider public to experience Filipino hospitality.

In addition to the independence day celebrations, the association organises the Salamat Po! Australia festival on Australia Day. This event offers a festive way for the Filipino community to express gratitude to Australia through inclusive multicultural activities. The group also coordinates regional events such as the Pasko sa Barrio Christmas festival, which features choral performances and local market vendors. Through these diverse initiatives, Barrio Fiesta Brisbane Inc actively preserves cultural traditions and fosters social integration in the local community.

name: Batch Cookie Bar x Lunar Pies
description:
Batch Cookie Bar x Lunar Pies is a popular bakehouse and empanada kiosk located at Uptown (Level E) in Brisbane City. Established in 2020 by Ginger Mendoza and Soren Andersen, this local favourite specialises in small-batch, handcrafted chunky cookies and gourmet Filipino-inspired pastries.

Dessert lovers highly recommend their signature Ube Velvet Crackle cookie, which features a soft, velvety texture. Other popular sweet treats include their buttermilk pancake, Dubai chocolate, and Choc Nut cookies. On the savoury side, customers enjoy their range of empanadas, such as the pork empanada and the creamy chicken Camembert and mushroom empanada, paired with a selection of dipping salsas. To complete the experience, they serve quality coffees, matcha lattes, and decadent milkshakes.

The kiosk operates on a takeaway-only basis, making it a convenient spot to grab a quick treat or a thoughtful gift. Customers frequently praise the enthusiastic and warm service from the friendly staff. As a sister brand to Lunar Pies, this unique collaboration brings a delicious fusion of sweet and savoury flavours to the heart of the Brisbane CBD.

name: Bayanihan Cargo Services
description:
Bayanihan Cargo Services Pty Ltd is a dedicated freight forwarding service. It has connected the Filipino community in Brisbane and regional Queensland with their loved ones since 2000. Carol and Raymond Johnson founded the business behind their suburban Brisbane sari-sari store. Today, the company continues their legacy by specialising in door-to-door sea and air cargo solutions.

They offer premium balikbayan box sea freight with no weight limits. They also provide air express cargo for parcels up to twenty kilograms. The team uses advanced tracking technology to keep senders and receivers updated at every stage.

Their network of local agents spans across Queensland. This includes suburbs like Inala and Mt Gravatt. It also covers regional hubs like Toowoomba, Townsville, and Cairns. This network makes it simple for families to send boxes of support back home.

Whether you are sending household items or personal gifts, they focus on friendly service. Their deep roots in the Filipino-Australian community ensure that each shipment is handled with care.

name: Best Friends Foods
description:
Best Friends Foods is a long-standing Filipino grocery store, bakery, and takeaway canteen located in Fortitude Valley's McWhirters Centre. Operating in Brisbane for over thirty years, this cosy shop specialises in authentic products and flavours from home.

Local shoppers appreciate the comprehensive selection of imported groceries, frozen goods, Filipino sausages, and pantry staples. The bakery counter is popular for its fresh, light, and delicious cakes, particularly custom ube cakes made for special occasions. On weekends, the takeaway food stall serves cafeteria-style hot dishes such as pork binagoogan, adobo, dinuguan, and pork sisig. Classic street foods like kwek-kwek served with vinegar and cucumber, as well as cassava cake desserts, are also highly recommended.

The shop is kept neat and tidy, though the aisles can be a bit compact during peak hours. Conveniently situated near major bus and train routes, it offers easy access for visitors, with main street parking nearby. The staff are generally welcoming, helping to create a familiar community hub for the Filipino diaspora and local food enthusiasts in Brisbane.

name: Black Eagle Arnis Escrima - Brisbane
description: Black Eagle Arnis Escrima Club is a martial arts club in Brisbane specialising in Arnis Escrima, the traditional weapons-based martial art of the Philippines, which is also commonly known as stickfighting. Founded in Cebu and brought to Australia, the club was established in 2009 by Master Juan Acevedo-Bravo at the request of Grandmaster Carlos Navarro. Training sessions focus on three core principles: speed, body movement, and sharpness of the eye. Students learn a variety of defensive and offensive techniques, including the use of empty hands, bladed weapons, and blunt sticks. The curriculum is designed to help students build coordination, visual alertness, and self-defence skills in a safe and supportive community environment. While the club traditionally teaches across base locations in Brisbane and the Gold Coast, it is currently closed for active training. Prospective students are encouraged to check their official website and Facebook page for future updates on when training will resume.

name: Brisbane Filipino Multicultural Seventh-day Adventist Church
description:
The Brisbane Filipino Multicultural Seventh-day Adventist Church is a welcoming Christian congregation that serves the Filipino community in Brisbane's northern suburbs. Meeting at the historic Toombul Shire Hall in Nundah, this active church centre provides a warm space for worship, learning, and fellowship.

Every Saturday, the community gathers for a range of family-friendly Sabbath programs. The morning begins with Sabbath School at 9:30 AM, followed by Bible study at 10:15 AM and the main divine worship service at 11:15 AM. A standout highlight of the weekly gathering is the shared potluck lunch at 12:00 PM, which allows members and visitors to connect over food. The day concludes with the youth-focused AY Program starting at 1:30 PM.

Worshippers frequently praise the church for its friendly and nurturing environment. Visitors often note the congregation's hospitality, describing it as a place where they feel immediately at home. With its combination of spiritual services and community-focused social activities, the church serves as an important spiritual and social hub for the local Filipino diaspora in Nundah and surrounding Brisbane suburbs.

name: Busog Sarap - Brisbane
description:
Busog Sarap is a popular Brisbane-based food business and pop-up street food stall that specialises in authentic Filipino delicacies. Originally launched as a food blog in 2009, the business has grown into a beloved local fixture. The food stall pops up on the first Saturday of the month at the Mt Gravatt Street Food markets at the Mount Gravatt Showgrounds.

The stall is renowned for its charcoal-grilled street food favourites. Customers can enjoy freshly grilled pork skewers, beef and pork isaw, kwek kwek, lumpia shanghai cups, and fish balls. They also offer traditional sweets and desserts, including yema cake tubs, boat tarts, and refreshing sago’t gulaman. Every dish is prepared in a licensed production kitchen, ensuring high-quality standards.

Locals highly recommend their signature Ilocos empanada, noting that the crispy, flavourful pastry is well worth the wait. Because the stall is a local favourite, it frequently draws long queues of hungry patrons. Arriving early to the Mt Gravatt Showgrounds is highly recommended to secure a spot in line and experience these authentic Filipino flavours.

name: Cafe de Manila
description:
Cafe de Manila is a traditional Filipino bakery and café located in McWhirter's Farmers Market at 232 Wickham Street in Fortitude Valley. As a cherished local favourite in the heart of Brisbane, this spot specialises in traditional Filipino breads, sweets, and pastries.

While the bakery primarily focuses on bulk orders for local Asian grocery stores, it also welcomes walk-in customers looking for a taste of home. Regulars highly recommend trying their signature ube cake, which is a popular choice for birthdays and special occasions. The bakery is also well-known for its fresh pandesal, sweet pastry rolls with caramel sauce, and delicious ensaymada. Customers appreciate the warm and friendly service, often noting the lovely owner who greets everyone with a smile.

Because Cafe de Manila focuses heavily on wholesale supply, walk-in stock can be limited. Placing orders in advance is highly recommended. The café is open daily, operating from 8:00 am to 4:30 pm on weekdays, and 8:00 am to 4:00 pm on weekends. It is a fantastic destination to discover authentic Filipino baked treats in Fortitude Valley.

name: Carlo’s Kitchen
description:
Carlo’s Kitchen is a welcoming, casual Filipino restaurant located at Mango Hill on Brisbane’s northside. Known for its warm hospitality, the eatery specialises in authentic comfort food that brings a taste of the Philippines to Queensland. The owner, Carlo, and his friendly staff are highly regarded by the community for their attentive service and personal touch in a relaxed alfresco dining atmosphere.

Locals rave about the restaurant’s signature dishes, particularly the tender chicken inasal and smoky BBQ chicken served with fragrant java rice. Other crowd-favourites include the rich bagnet kare-kare, gotong batangas, crispy pata, and lechon kawali. Families appreciate the kid-friendly offerings, such as the Jollibee-style fried chicken and sweet spaghetti, which are big hits with children. Portions are generous and perfect for sharing, and the kitchen also prepares large party trays for special celebrations.

Whether you choose to dine in the outdoor seating area or order takeaway, the restaurant provides a comforting experience. They open daily from 11:00 am to 9:00 pm, offering delicious meals at affordable prices. Carlo’s Kitchen also runs a daily home delivery service between 5:00 pm and 7:00 pm for the local Moreton Bay community.

name: Cebuano Nomads Cleaning Services
description: Cebuano Nomads Cleaning Services is a dynamic and reliable professional cleaning company based in Brisbane, Queensland. Inspired by their rich Cebuano heritage, the friendly team specialises in delivering high-standard residential and commercial cleaning, pest control, and Defence Housing Australia (DHA) bond cleans. Clients appreciate their focus on eco-friendly practices, exceptional reliability, and meticulous attention to detail. Whether maintaining a busy family home or preparing a commercial property for hand-over, the experienced team is dedicated to leaving every space spotless and refreshed. Operating under a strong ethos of community connection, they constantly modernise their techniques to ensure consistent, premium results. Known for their approachable and helpful service, the team works hard to exceed expectations, building long-term relationships with Brisbane residents and local businesses. From regular house cleaning to specialised exit cleans, Cebuano Nomads provides efficient, high-quality cleaning solutions that help maintain a healthy, clean, and happy lifestyle for the local Brisbane community.

name: Chook'z Master
description:
Chook'z Master was a beloved Filipino eatery located inside Inala Plaza in Inala, Brisbane. Known for bringing authentic tastes of the Philippines to the local community, the restaurant specialised in a range of comforting traditional dishes.

Customers frequently praised their signature Filipino-style fried chicken, which was celebrated for being exceptionally crunchy, juicy, and beautifully seasoned with a hint of garlic. Another crowd favourite was their sweet Filipino-style spaghetti, featuring a rich, cheesy tomato sauce with hot dog slices that transported many diners back to their childhood. The menu also featured generous portions of crispy chicken skin, flavourful beef stew balbacua with lemongrass, savoury pancit bihon guisado, and authentic sisig. For dessert, visitors enjoyed traditional treats like halo-halo and banana fritters served with ice cream.

With its budget-friendly prices and warm, smiling staff who provided helpful service even during the busiest lunch hours, Chook'z Master was a popular casual dining spot for the Filipino-Australian community. This business has permanently closed.

name: Christ Connect Church
description:
Christ Connect Church is a welcoming Christian fellowship serving the Filipino-Australian community in Arana Hills and across the Brisbane area. As an active member of the Australian Christian Churches network, the congregation specialises in bringing people together for worship, fellowship, and spiritual growth. The church meets every Sunday at 9:30 AM for worship services at the Arana Hills Community Centre on Dawson Parade.

Locals value the church for its genuine hospitality, warm friendship, and uplifting atmosphere. Attendees frequently highlight the inspiring, biblically grounded messages and the strong sense of family within the congregation. It is a supportive space where members find care, understanding, and encouragement in their faith journey.

Beyond Sunday services, the church organises active ministries for different groups, including a dedicated young adults Bible study and couples fellowship nights. Senior Pastor Pete Caiga and his wife Tweet lead the congregation, which also streams services online for those unable to attend in person. The community is active in local outreach, supporting youth programmes and charitable efforts to assist the homeless in Brisbane.

name: Christ's Commission Fellowship
description:
Christ's Commission Fellowship Brisbane is a welcoming Christian church community situated in Gaythorne. As part of a global movement, the fellowship focuses on spiritual growth, discipleship, and community engagement. They help individuals build a closer connection to faith through active group participation and mentorship.

The church holds its weekly worship services every Sunday at 10:00 AM in the main auditorium of the QB Centre on Prospect Road. The venue is open on Sundays from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM to accommodate various group fellowships and community activities.

Attendees highly appreciate the warm and family-friendly atmosphere of the congregation. Many visitors note that the community is exceptionally welcoming, making it easy for newcomers to feel at home and form meaningful relationships. The fellowship offers a supportive environment where people can share in prayer, study scripture, and encourage one another. For those looking to join, the church can be contacted via email at ccfbrisbane@gmail.com or by phone at 0411 614 108. You can also find more details on their official website at ccfaustralia.com.au.

name: Clontarf Beach Baptist Church - Filipino Ministry
description:
Clontarf Beach Baptist Church in Clontarf, Queensland, hosts a vibrant and dedicated Filipino ministry that welcomes everyone on the Redcliffe Peninsula. Led by Pastor PJ Edu, this ministry organises the Simbahang Filipino worship service every Sunday at 11:00 am. It provides a wonderful opportunity for the Filipino-Australian community to praise God, connect, and support one another.

Locals and visitors frequently praise the church for its friendly congregation and warm, family-like atmosphere. Newcomers find it easy to feel at home here, as members are always happy to welcome and chat with new attendees. Beyond spiritual services, the church specialises in practical community support. Every Friday evening at 5:30 pm, they serve a free, high-quality community meal to assist the poor, lonely, and homeless in the local area.

Whether you want to join the Sunday worship service, participate in growth groups, or connect with a caring community, the Clontarf Beach Baptist Church Filipino ministry offers a supportive centre for faith and multicultural fellowship.

name: Coffee Mentality Roastery - Woolloongabba
description:
Coffee Mentality Roastery in Woolloongabba is a gold-medal-winning specialty coffee roaster and espresso bar. Founded by Ian Abadiano, an award-winning Filipino-Australian migrant, this warm community hub specialises in sustainable coffee practices and coffee education. Ian maintains strong connections to his heritage by sourcing premium coffee beans directly from growers in the Philippines through the 'Pinagmulaan' project, supporting local farmers.

At the Woolloongabba headquarters, visitors can experience a welcoming and pet-friendly environment. Coffee lovers can select their preferred single-origin beans for a custom brew. The passionate staff are always enthusiastic to introduce different varieties, crafting velvet-smooth lattes with beautiful art. The roastery is highly regarded for its educational workshops, including popular barista classes led by Brad and hand-pour coffee cupping sessions.

Whether you are popping in for a smooth hand-pour, purchasing award-winning beans, or attending an engaging tasting masterclass, the roastery provides a truly premium coffee experience. It is the perfect spot in Brisbane to enjoy exceptional specialty brews while supporting sustainable, ethical farming.

name: Coffee Rounds Cafe
description:
Coffee Rounds Cafe is a warm and welcoming family-owned Filipino café in Redcliffe, situated directly opposite the Redcliffe Hospital. Run by local community staples Genesis and Pam, this cosy venue has built a popular following among hospital staff and residents for its friendly service and relaxing atmosphere. The café features a side room and stage that supports local live music, creating a fantastic community vibe.

Unlike standard coffee shops, a dedicated chef cooks high-quality, hot meals in the kitchen. The menu offers a delightful blend of traditional Australian breakfast choices and authentic Filipino street food. Customer favourites include freshly made bibingka cooked with care, lumpia vegetable wraps, pork skewers with rice, and classic sisig. For dessert, the café specialises in halo-halo made with real ube ice cream, along with delicious smoothies and artisan coffee from the Moreton Bay Coffee Company. Whether you are popping in for a quick takeaway or enjoying a relaxed dine-in lunch, Coffee Rounds Cafe offers a wonderful taste of the Philippines with outstanding hospitality.

name: Dovetail Social
description:
Dovetail Social is a warm and welcoming gluten-free café located in the leafy suburb of Highgate Hill, Brisbane. Co-owned by award-winning Filipino-Australian pastry chef Rejoice Thomson, the venue specialises in blending classic Australian brunch favourites with rich Filipino flavours.

Local visitors frequently praise the café for its cosy atmosphere and highly accommodating service, making it a reliable destination for those with strict coeliac or dairy-free requirements. The menu features unique sweet creations, including the signature purple ube pavlova donuts, ube leche flan cakes, and the famous 'Yamingtons'—ube-flavoured lamingtons. On the savoury side, diners can enjoy comforting chicken or pork adobo bowls, crispy chicken waffles, and a unique gluten-free high tea experience. A selection of specialty coffee from local roasters completes the menu.

As a sister café to Norman Park's Dovetail on Overend, this neighbourhood spot offers a cosy courtyard space perfect for a relaxing brunch. It has established itself as a beloved community hub where friends and family can gather to share authentic and inclusive dining experiences.

name: Dr Viola Laurel - Queen And Albert Dental
description:
Dr Viola Laurel is a compassionate, multilingual dentist practising at Queen & Albert Dental in the heart of Brisbane City. Originally from the Philippines, she moved to Queensland and completed her Master of Dentistry at Griffith University on the Gold Coast in 2020. Dr Laurel has a decade of prior experience working as an oral health therapist, which shapes her gentle and patient-centred clinical approach.

At the modern, welcoming Brisbane CBD clinic, Dr Laurel specialises in a broad range of dental care. Her areas of expertise include general check-ups, endodontics, periodontics, and restorative and cosmetic treatments. She focuses on creating personalised treatment plans that prioritise patient comfort and long-term oral health.

Patients value the clinic's calm and stress-free environment, designed to ease dental anxiety. Dr Laurel is fluent in English, Tagalog, and Cebuano, allowing her to deliver culturally sensitive care to Brisbane's Filipino-Australian community. Her ability to explain procedures clearly in multiple languages helps patients feel fully informed and relaxed during their appointments.

name: ERMA Asian Shop
description:
ERMA Asian Shop, also warmly known by locals as Ate Imelda's shop, is a welcoming, women-owned Asian grocery store located in Lawnton, in northern Brisbane. Situated directly across the road from the Lawnton train station and close to the UniSC Moreton Bay campus, this store is a beloved local hub for the Filipino community.

The shop features a fantastic and diverse selection of authentic Filipino groceries, ingredients, and hidden treats that help transport homesick visitors back to the Philippines. Customers will find a comprehensive range of packaged, canned, bottled, bagged, and frozen food items perfect for cooking traditional recipes at home.

Visitors consistently praise the store's reasonable prices and the exceptionally friendly, hospitable service provided by Ate Imelda. The store is well-organised and clean, making it easy to browse the extensive variety of stock. Whether you are looking for specific pantry essentials or fresh Calamansi seedlings, this hidden gem offers a delightful shopping experience.

name: Eagle Cargo
description:
Eagle Cargo is a trusted logistics provider that specialises in door-to-door balikbayan box and sea freight services from Queensland to the Philippines. Based in Brisbane, they connect Filipino families across Australia by delivering care packages to their loved ones back home. The company offers a highly convenient service, including an easy online booking system and downloadable customs forms.

Local customers appreciate their flexible schedule, which features standard weekday hours along with weekend availability and after-hours pickup options. For added convenience, Eagle Cargo partners with several local Filipino groceries across Brisbane and the Gold Coast for easy box drop-off and pickup. These include Kabayan Filipino Store branches in Acacia Ridge, Wishart, Chermside, and Browns Plains, as well as Best Friends Filipino Grocery in Fortitude Valley.

Clients highly recommend their services for their reliability and excellent communication, noting that the team keeps them updated throughout the transit process. The shipments are handled with great care, ensuring that packages consistently arrive in perfect condition. With regular schedules and a focus on community trust, they make shipping cargo simple and stress-free.

name: El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Brisbane
description:
El Shaddai DWXI PPFI Brisbane is a vibrant Filipino Catholic charismatic prayer group based in Daisy Hill. Operating as a local chapter of the international El Shaddai movement, the group brings together the Filipino-Australian community for weekly fellowship, worship, and spiritual encouragement.

Gatherings take place every Sunday afternoon at St Edward the Confessor Catholic Church, offering a welcoming space for prayer and song. Community members value the group for its supportive environment, where individuals can experience spiritual renewal and healing. Meetings are typically structured around lively praise and worship sessions, scripture readings, and shared testimonies, and are followed by a warm community fellowship.

Beyond their regular Sunday assemblies, the Brisbane chapter actively participates in wider parish life, including assisting with liturgical music and parish hosting. The group is locally led by coordinators who facilitate outreach and support for members across the region. Whether you are seeking a prayerful sanctuary, traditional charismatic worship, or a connection to the Filipino Catholic community, El Shaddai Brisbane welcomes all parishioners and visitors to join their weekly assemblies.

name: FABS Filipino Australian Brisbane Society Inc.
description:
The Filipino Australian Brisbane Society Inc. (FABS) is a registered non-profit community organisation dedicated to serving the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane and Southeast Queensland. Established in 2019, the society focuses on promoting and preserving Filipino cultural heritage, language, and values, helping newly arrived migrants settle and fostering connections across generations.

The group is best known for organising major cultural celebrations, most notably the annual Bayanihan Festival in June. Held at the Kingston Butter Factory Cultural Precinct, this popular festival celebrates Philippine Independence Day with vibrant cultural dances, live music, and authentic Filipino food stalls. Community feedback highlight the festival as a welcoming space that brings together diverse multicultural groups. FABS also organises other key events throughout the year, including the Buwan ng Wika cultural activities in August and the Pasko at Bayle Christmas celebrations in December.

Through active collaboration with local councils, artists, and volunteers, the society serves as a key cultural hub. They invite everyone to participate in their events, volunteer, or join as members to celebrate and share Filipino culture in Queensland.

name: FAST Productions
description:
FAST Productions is a premier live entertainment and event promotion agency based in the Brisbane and Gold Coast region of Queensland. The team specialises in bringing world-class Filipino artists, musicians, and performers to audiences across Australia. They manage the entire event lifecycle, including artist bookings, event production, venue coordination, local marketing, and ticketing through their dedicated online platform, FAST Events.

Filipino-Australian community members highly appreciate the company for staging major cultural events and concerts. FAST Productions has successfully promoted and produced shows for legendary Original Pilipino Music (OPM) icons and popular entertainment acts, including the Eraserheads, Moira Dela Torre, TJ Monterde, Silent Sanctuary, and Sunkissed Lola. By organising these national tours and comedy showcases, they provide a vital cultural connection for the diaspora. Whether hosting large-scale concerts at central Brisbane arenas or intimate community showcases, FAST Productions focuses on creating professional, memorable experiences that celebrate Filipino culture and talent down under.

name: FILOZ UQ
description:
FilOz UQ, also known as the Association of Filipinos and Filipino-Australians at the University of Queensland, is a vibrant student-led society based at the St Lucia campus in Brisbane. The group focuses on creating a welcoming community for Filipino international students, postgraduates, and anyone interested in Filipino culture, helping members feel at home while studying in Australia.

Members highly value the club for its warm hospitality, cultural connection, and supportive environment. FilOz UQ organise a wide range of social events and activities throughout the academic year. Key offerings include student networking nights, annual balls, collaborative study sessions, and casual social gatherings. They also run active community initiatives, such as charity relief drives to assist natural disaster victims in the Philippines.

New members can easily get involved by signing up for the annual club membership, which offers benefits like discounts with local business partners such as Upbeat Cafe. Based in the heart of UQ, the association serves as an essential cultural bridge and support network for the Brisbane Filipino-Australian student community.

name: Favor Church Brisbane
description:
Favor Church Brisbane is a vibrant, non-denominational church community located in Carindale, Queensland. Originally established as a plant from Favor Church in Manila, Philippines, the Brisbane campus is led by Pastors Ben and Amy Dolling. Embodying their motto as a 'church for imperfect people,' the congregation focuses on building a welcoming environment where everyone feels like family.

Attendees regularly highlight the warm welcome team, the passionate worship, and the joyful music. The church offers inspiring Bible-based sermons and places a strong emphasis on community care and discipleship. Key ministries include a wonderful kids program (Favor Kids), an engaging youth group, and regular connection groups for teens, young adults, and families. Services are held on Sundays at 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM.

With its deep cultural and spiritual ties to its sister congregation in the Philippines, the church serves as a supportive bridge for the Filipino-Australian community. It provides a nurturing space to connect, grow, and refresh your faith in a friendly atmosphere.

name: Feast North Brisbane
description:
Feast North Brisbane is a vibrant Filipino Catholic prayer gathering and lay community located in Aspley. Operating under the umbrella of the international Light of Jesus Family founded by Bro Bo Sanchez, this welcoming congregation specialises in providing a warm space for spiritual enrichment, fellowship, and faith talks. The group's primary mission is to shine God's light by forming disciples who love God, love others, and bless the wider community.

Gatherings are held monthly on the second Saturday of the month from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM, hosted within the peaceful surroundings of Our Lady and St. Dympna's Parish on Robinson Road West. Attendees can expect inspiring worship sessions, faith-filled discussions, and a strong sense of community support. The active Young Adults ministry is a key highlight, organising inspiring initiatives like the Love Life Retreat to encourage spiritual growth among youth. Whether you are seeking a supportive faith family or looking to participate in monthly worship, Feast North Brisbane offers a welcoming spiritual centre for all.

name: Feast South Brisbane
description:
Feast South Brisbane is a warm and welcoming Filipino Catholic prayer gathering and lay community operating under the international Light of Jesus Family. Originally founded by brother Bo Sanchez, this active community specialises in offering a vibrant and nurturing environment for spiritual growth, charismatic praise, and heartfelt fellowship. Guided by their mission to shine God's light, they focus on making passionate disciples who love God, support others, and bless the wider community.

The group meets monthly, typically convening on the fourth Saturday of the month from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM. During these monthly gatherings, attendees participate in uplifting worship music, listen to inspiring talks, and engage in small group discussions. These events are commonly hosted at friendly local venues in the southern suburbs of Brisbane, such as the Avocado Room or Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic Church in Sunnybank. This provides a central and accessible meeting point for the Filipino-Australian diaspora to gather. Everyone is welcome to join these sessions, which offer a supportive space for individuals and young adults looking to deepen their faith, build meaningful friendships, and celebrate their shared cultural and spiritual heritage in Queensland.

name: Federation of Filipino Communities in Queensland Inc.
description:
The Federation of Filipino Communities in Queensland Inc. (commonly known as FedFilQ) is a dedicated non-profit peak body serving the Filipino-Australian community across Queensland. Based in the Brisbane suburb of Newmarket, the organisation focuses on uniting local Filipino groups, promoting cultural heritage, and establishing strong community advocacy.

FedFilQ is highly regarded for coordinating major cultural events. Their most prominent activity is the annual Barrio Fiesta Brisbane, a celebrated Philippine cultural festival held at the Rocklea Showgrounds. This event showcases traditional Filipino cuisine, live musical performances, and vibrant folk dances, welcoming both the diaspora and the wider public to celebrate Philippine Independence Day.

The federation plays a key role in capacity building and collaboration. They foster linkages with external organisations and help newly arrived Filipino migrants establish local support networks. By providing a collective voice and organising inclusive cultural celebrations, FedFilQ ensures that Filipino traditions remain active and cherished in Queensland. For enquiries, the association can be contacted directly on their community number.

name: FilOz Cuisine
description:
FilOz Cuisine is a welcoming, family-oriented Filipino restaurant located at Lincoln Street in Strathpine, north of Brisbane. The cosy eatery specialises in serving authentic Filipino comfort food, providing a homey atmosphere complete with traditional decor and background music featuring popular Filipino singers.

Diners highly recommend the restaurant for its friendly service and quick preparation times, making it a local favourite for both dining in and taking away. The menu showcases classic dishes such as sizzling pork sisig, crispy pata pork hock, lechon kawali, and beef caldereta. Seafood lovers can enjoy fried bangus or fresh tilapia, while those with a sweet tooth can choose from desserts like ube ice cream, ume cake, and traditional halo-halo.

Families appreciate the hospitable staff and the generous portion sizes, which are perfect for sharing with groups. Whether you are familiar with Filipino cuisine or trying it for the first time, this humble venue offers a comforting taste of home-style cooking that is sure to satisfy.

name: Filipino Catholic Fellowship - Living Waters Parish
description:
Living Waters Catholic Parish in Caboolture serves as a vibrant spiritual home and community centre for the local Filipino-Australian community. Gathers regularly at St Peter's Catholic Church, the parish specialises in providing a warm, friendly, and inclusive space where everyone is welcomed instantly as part of the family. The parish offers scriptural-based services and a casual kids club that focuses on teaching biblical parables with the help of dedicated volunteers.

Families frequently organise christening ceremonies and sacramental blessings here, appreciating the helpfulness and patience of the priests and parish staff. The local St Vincent de Paul conference also operates within the parish, supporting community welfare projects and hosting successful charity fundraisers. Located conveniently close to the Caboolture train station, the venue features clean toilet facilities, a spacious car park, and a separate celebration area ideal for anniversaries, birthdays, and community gatherings. Through its regular Filipino Mass and fellowship events, Living Waters Parish continues to foster a strong sense of cultural connection and spiritual support for Filipino families across the region.

name: Filipino Mass Community - St Patrick's Church
description: The Filipino Mass Community at St Patrick's Church in Fortitude Valley offers a welcoming spiritual home for the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane. Celebrated on the first Sunday of each month at 12:00 PM, the services feature Tagalog liturgy and beautiful traditional hymns sung by the Filipino Nundah Choir. Parishioners and visitors highly appreciate the warm, inclusive, and historic environment of the church. The historic building itself is a cherished landmark in the valley, featuring stunning stained glass windows and a peaceful, reverent atmosphere that makes it a true hidden gem for worship. The local community and parish team work hard to ensure services are warm and accommodating. Whether you are looking to join in the monthly mass celebrations, hear the wonderful choir, or simply experience the historical beauty of the church, this community provides a wonderful space to connect with faith and culture.

name: Filipino Sports Connect Inc.
description:
Filipino Sports Connect Inc. is a community-focused sports organisation based in Brisbane, Queensland. The group specialises in bringing the local Filipino-Australian community together through sporting activities, events, and community-driven initiatives. By promoting active participation and sportsmanship, the organisation helps community members build strong connections and foster camaraderie in a welcoming environment.

A key highlight of their annual calendar is the Mabuhay Cup Charity Golf Day. Hosted at the premier Indooroopilly Golf Club, this flagship tournament serves as a celebration of community, sport, and business networking. The event is organised in close partnership with the Philippine Consulate in Queensland. It raises funds for worthy humanitarian causes, such as supporting Destiny Rescue, whilst celebrating key milestones in Australia-Philippines diplomatic relations.

This organisation offers a fantastic platform to get involved. It welcomes avid golfers wanting to play in the Mabuhay Cup, as well as anyone wishing to connect through sports. For enquiries about registration or upcoming activities, you can reach out to the organisers directly by email or phone.

name: Filipino pearly buns
description:
Filipino Pearly Buns is a welcoming family-run bakery based in Bellbird Park, Queensland, that specialises in authentic Filipino steamed buns, traditionally known as siopao. The team prides itself on using a cherished family recipe passed down through generations, crafting each bun by hand. Local customers highly praise the exceptionally soft, fluffy, and pillowy texture of the bread, which complements their rich and savoury fillings. Popular menu favourites include the classic pork asado, chicken, and bola-bola (meatball) buns, along with sweet treats like leche flan cakes, ensaymada, and chiffon cake.

While their physical storefront is tucked away at Brent Lane in Bellbird Park, the bakery also operates as a popular stallholder at various Brisbane markets. They regularly serve the community at the Saturday Fresh Market in Rocklea from 6:00 AM to 12:00 PM, and make regular appearances at the Brisbane City Markets. Their friendly, hospitable service and fresh, high-quality offerings make Filipino Pearly Buns a popular destination for anyone seeking comfort food and an authentic taste of home in Queensland.

name: Filipino-Australian Business, Industry & Communities Council of QLD Inc.
description:
The Filipino-Australian Business, Industry & Communities Council of QLD Inc. (FABICCQ) is a dedicated non-profit community organisation representing and supporting Queensland-based Filipino businesses and communities. Established in January 2018, FABICCQ serves as the official state affiliate representing Queensland to the Filipino Communities Council of Australia (FILCCA), the national peak umbrella body.

The council actively works to foster a strong, united, and active Filipino-Australian community across the region. It focuses on supporting the professional, mental, and physical growth of both its members and the wider public. By offering a vital networking platform, advocacy, and cultural support, FABICCQ helps local businesses with Filipino affiliations connect and thrive. Although associated with Darling Heights in the Toowoomba region, the organisation extends its services, advocacy, and cultural preservation initiatives throughout Queensland. Through its active communication channels and community involvement, FABICCQ remains a key pillar of support for the Filipino diaspora in the Sunshine State.

name: Filipino-Australian Foundation of Queensland Inc.
description:
The Filipino-Australian Foundation of Queensland Inc. (FAFQ) serves as a welcoming community hub and a support network for Filipino migrants in Queensland. Located at the Philippine Multi-Purpose Centre in Coopers Plains, Brisbane, the foundation is dedicated to providing valuable settlement resources, community assistance, and educational scholarships that help individuals transition smoothly to life in Australia.

Community members praise the organisation for its warm, family-like atmosphere, with volunteers highlighting how the foundation fosters teamwork, cooperation, and pride in Filipino cultural heritage. A key pillar of the organisation is its highly regarded Grant-in-Aid Scholarship program, which has supported hundreds of underprivileged but deserving students in achieving their educational goals. Through cultural pageants and regular events, FAFQ helps connect the Filipino diaspora while fostering integration and unity within the broader multicultural community in Queensland. The leadership is widely commended for their generous, hands-on support and dedication to helping those in need.

name: Filipino-Australian Teachers Association of Queensland Inc.
description:
Established in 1995, the Filipino-Australian Teachers Association of Queensland Inc. (FATAQ) is a dedicated non-profit community organisation. It serves educators of Filipino background and supports the broader community across Southeast Queensland. The association focuses on promoting and preserving Filipino cultural heritage and language literacy.

FATAQ offers engaging Filipino language and culture classes tailored for learners of all ages. A key initiative is their Balikturo program. Through this program, the organisation raises funds to donate educational resources and share professional knowledge with schools in need in the Philippines.

The association is highly active in the community, regularly hosting information sessions, cultural events, and professional celebrations. Notable activities include World Teacher's Day celebrations, tree-planting projects, and annual general meetings. They also coordinate major milestones, such as their recent 30th-anniversary gala. These events bring together members to socialise, collaborate, and plan strategic community initiatives.

For educators and community members looking to connect, learn, or support educational development, FATAQ provides a welcoming and professional network in Queensland.

name: Filta
description:
Filta is a professional recruitment and staffing solutions agency located in the heart of Brisbane City. They specialise in nearshoring and outsourcing, helping businesses build and manage dedicated offshore teams in the Philippines and Colombia. They focus on digital, e-commerce, advertising, and media roles, ensuring local compliance, payroll, and HR support are handled properly.

Clients consistently praise the team for their exceptional attention to detail, genuine care, and clear communication. Reviewers highlight how easy and enjoyable the recruitment process is, noting that Filta helps them scale their teams with confidence while bridging any cultural differences. The agency acts as a true resourcing partner rather than a simple recruitment service.

With their physical office located on Edward Street in Brisbane, they are well-placed to support local Queensland businesses looking to grow their global workforce. Whether you need software developers, designers, or e-commerce specialists, Filta offers reliable, professional, and ethical outsourcing services tailored to your specific business requirements.

name: Flaglines
description:
Flaglines is a specialised employment and placement agency based in the heart of Brisbane City. Originally founded in 2010 as a training and consulting firm, the organisation transitioned in 2018 to focus on professional recruitment and workforce integration for the multicultural community.

The agency specialises in connecting qualified overseas Filipino skilled workers, professionals, and international students with reputable Australian businesses. They also assist newly arrived migrant jobseekers in finding rewarding career paths. By collaborating closely with employers, Flaglines helps candidates secure jobs, develop clear career pathways, and adapt to their new workplaces.

In addition to job matching, Flaglines provides essential migration and rural relocation support. These services help candidates settle smoothly into their new local areas. The agency operates across Brisbane and the wider South East Queensland region, including Logan, the Lockyer Valley, the Gold Coast, and the Moreton Region. You can find their main office located within the Fishburners co-working space on Queen Street.

name: Flavours of Asia
description:
Flavours of Asia, also known as Katuparan Filipino Restaurant, is a combined grocery store and eatery in Capalaba. Located at 125 Old Cleveland Road, it serves as a welcoming hub for the local community with easy parking around the back.

The grocery section specialises in essential Filipino ingredients and pantry staples, including Knorr liquid seasoning, bagoong, Mang Tomas, and Mama Sita flavour sachets. Out back, the kitchen serves a range of authentic home-cooked Filipino dishes. Customers love the generous portions and budget-friendly prices. Popular menu highlights include pancit bihon, kare-kare, crispy pata, roast pork, and beef pares. For dessert, visitors highly recommend the refreshing halo-halo, bibingkang malagkit with latik, and banana cue.

The owner, Cecile, and the cook, Jasper, are known for their friendly service and helpful cooking advice. Because the staff multitask between the cashier and the kitchen, there can sometimes be a slight wait during busy periods. However, the quality, value, and authentic street-style atmosphere make it a favourite local spot to get a Filipino food fix.

name: Forex Cargo Queensland
description:
Forex Cargo Queensland is a dedicated cargo forwarding and logistics provider that specialises in sending balikbayan boxes from Queensland to the Philippines. Serving the local Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane, the Gold Coast, and regional areas, they offer both sea and air freight door-to-door delivery options. The company provides premium, high-quality empty boxes designed with a reinforced base to carry heavy loads and keep fragile items safe during transit.

Many long-term customers appreciate the convenient home collection service, which is run by helpful local pickup staff. While logistics timelines can experience delays during peak periods like Christmas, the service remains highly regarded for its overall reliability and care. Customers can easily track the progress of their shipments online using the web tracking system for added peace of mind. For enquiries, bookings, or box deliveries, Queensland residents can contact their local team directly by phone or email. This cargo service plays a vital role in helping local families stay connected by delivering love and packages directly to relatives back in the Philippines.

name: Genesis Filipino Wholesale and Retail Shop
description:
Genesis Filipino Wholesale and Retail Shop was a local grocery store located on Prosperity Place in Geebung, Brisbane. It specialised in offering a wide selection of authentic Filipino food products, packaged ingredients, and popular groceries to the local community. Customers frequently visited the shop for both wholesale and retail options, which made traditional ingredients highly accessible and affordable for families and home cooks. 

The shop was well-regarded in the area for its warm and welcoming environment. Visitors often praised the friendly staff and kind customer service, which created a pleasant shopping experience for anyone seeking a taste of the Philippines. It served as a helpful hub in the northern suburbs of Brisbane, providing people with convenient access to familiar pantry staples, snacks, and specialty items imported from overseas. 

Whether customers were shopping in bulk for their households or picking up a few retail treats, the staff made sure everyone felt welcome.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Hapag Kainan
description:
Hapag Kainan is an authentic Filipino eatery located within the vibrant 8 Street dining precinct at Westfield Mt Gravatt in Upper Mount Gravatt. Operating in a lively, hawker-style market space, it serves a diverse range of traditional Filipino dishes to local shoppers and food enthusiasts.

Customers appreciate the casual dining atmosphere and the convenience of its shopping centre location. The menu features classic Pinoy dishes, including flavourful pork sisig, savoury bulalo beef soup, crispy pata, and a selection of popular grilled items like pork barbecue skewers. It also specialises in all-day breakfast plates, such as bangsilog and tapsilog, which are served with garlic fried rice and egg. Traditional desserts like turon and sweet leche flan are also available.

While the eatery is a popular spot for quick and satisfying meals, some patrons note that pricing is on the higher side and portion sizes for certain dishes can be small. Nevertheless, it remains a go-to destination in Brisbane's southern suburbs for anyone seeking authentic and hearty Filipino flavours.

name: Harrison International Recruitment Agency
description:
Harrison International Recruitment Agency (HIRA) is a family-owned offshore staffing provider based in Brisbane, Queensland. The agency specialises in connecting Australian businesses with skilled professionals and virtual assistants from the Philippines. It prioritises ethical recruitment practices, ensuring that Filipino contractors receive fair contracts, flexible working shifts, and compensation that is meaningfully above local Philippine market rates.

Transparency is a core value at HIRA. The agency operates on a clear flat-margin pricing structure with zero hidden contractor fees. To ensure long-term recruitment success, HIRA offers a thirty-day replacement guarantee on all placements at no additional cost to the client.

Beyond staffing, the agency is committed to making a positive social impact. It pledges ten per cent of its annual profits to registered charities that address food insecurity and poverty in both Australia and the Philippines. As a local Brisbane family business, HIRA brings honesty, integrity, and ethical standards to the offshore recruitment sector, offering a reliable partnership for businesses seeking to expand their teams with offshore talent.

name: IGLESIA NI CRISTO [Queensland District Office] - Brisbane
description:
Iglesia Ni Cristo, located in Coopers Plains, serves as the Queensland District Office and Brisbane locale for the Church of Christ. This place of worship provides a spiritual home and a welcoming space for the local Filipino-Australian Christian community, hosting regular congregational services in both English and Tagalog.

Attendees frequently appreciate the warm hospitality of the congregation and the solemn, peaceful atmosphere during services. The church features a dedicated choir known for its beautiful blending, enhancing the worship experience. Located at 65 Orange Grove Road, the venue offers convenient facilities, including a large rear parking area to accommodate members and visitors.

Worship services are held mid-week and on weekends to suit different schedules. Weekly English services take place on Wednesday evenings at 7:30 pm, Thursday mornings at 6:00 am, and Saturday mornings and evenings at 7:30 am and 6:30 pm respectively. Additionally, a Tagalog service is conducted on the first Saturday of each month at 7:30 am. This locale continues to play a vital role in fostering faith and strong community ties among its members in the greater Brisbane region.

name: IMA Filipino Foods
description:
IMA Filipino Foods is a family-run wholesale distributor specialised in supplying authentic Filipino and Asian food products. Located in Caboolture, Queensland, the business has spent over twenty years serving the local community, catering to emerging Asian grocery shops, established retailers, and local restaurants.

Customers value the warehouse for offering some of the most affordable prices in the region, providing great value alongside a delicious selection of items. The extensive inventory includes pantry staples, specialty ingredients, frozen goods, cosmetics, and popular sweet treats like leche flan cake sandwiches and sweetened condensed creamer. By supplying these authentic products, IMA Filipino Foods helps local families and businesses enjoy and share the unique flavours of the Philippines.

With a commitment to excellent customer service and reliable delivery options, this warehouse is a key supplier for the Filipino-Australian community in Queensland and northern New South Wales. Whether you are running a retail shop or stocking a restaurant kitchen, IMA Filipino Foods makes it easy to source your favourite ingredients.

name: Iglesia Ni Cristo - Locale of Brisbane
description: Located in Coopers Plains, the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church of Christ) Brisbane Locale serves as the ecclesiastical district office for Queensland and a central place of worship for the local Filipino-Australian community. Members and visitors describe the house of worship as a solemn, peaceful, and welcoming sanctuary that fosters a strong sense of fellowship. The congregation is highly regarded for its warm community spirit, friendly attendees, and a dedicated choir that delivers beautifully blended hymns. The chapel offers a generous parking space at the back of the property, providing easy access for the congregation during events. Under the guidance of Resident Minister Glenn B. David, the locale hosts regular worship services throughout the week. Weekly English services take place on Wednesday at 7:30 PM, Thursday at 6:00 AM, Saturday at 7:30 AM and 6:30 PM, and Sunday at 8:00 AM. A Tagalog service is also held every first Saturday of the month at 7:30 AM, and a Children's Worship Service (CWS) runs on Sunday at 10:00 AM. This active schedule ensures that all members of the community can participate in worship and find spiritual encouragement.

name: Iglesia Ni Cristo - Locale of Caboolture
description:
Iglesia Ni Cristo - Locale of Caboolture is a welcoming Christian congregation that serves the Filipino-Australian community in the Moreton Bay region. As part of the global Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church of Christ), which has its historical roots in the Philippines, this local church offers a supportive spiritual centre for believers in Caboolture and the surrounding northern areas of Greater Brisbane.

The congregation gathers for regular worship services at the Caboolture Combined Services Hall, situated at 21 Hayes Street, Caboolture. These gatherings are held in English on Tuesday evenings at 7:30 PM and Friday evenings at 7:30 PM. These mid-week and end-of-week services provide local members with valuable opportunities for spiritual devotion, prayer, and mutual encouragement.

For those looking to connect or learn more about the church's activities, the local congregation is guided by dedicated leaders, including Head Deacon Frankie Cruzpero and Resident Minister Arnel Sembrano. The Locale of Caboolture focuses on fostering deep community connections, offering pastoral support, and helping families grow together in their faith. Everyone in the local community is welcome to attend and participate in the worship services.

name: Iglesia Ni Cristo - Locale of North Brisbane
description:
The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church of Christ) Locale of North Brisbane is a welcoming Christian congregation serving the local Filipino-Australian and wider community. Situated in Shop 6 at 545 Roghan Road within the Fitzgibbon Community Centre precinct, this church offers a central space for spiritual growth, fellowship, and worship in the northern suburbs of Brisbane.

The locale hosts regular worship services conducted in English to ensure accessibility for all attendees. Services are held mid-week on Wednesday evenings at 7:00 PM, and on weekends on Saturday evenings at 7:00 PM. Additionally, the congregation provides a dedicated Children’s Worship Service (CWS) on Saturdays at 5:00 PM, helping younger members engage with their faith.

Pastoral leadership is provided by Resident Minister Arnel Sembrano, alongside Head Deacon Paul Quiday and Head Secretary Liberty Taruc. Together, they guide the congregation in regular Bible studies, community outreach, and spiritual activities. Whether you are a local resident or visiting the region, the locale offers a supportive environment to participate in worship and connect with the community.

name: Iglesia Ni Cristo [Queensland] - Locale of Logan
description:
Iglesia Ni Cristo Locale of Logan is a Christian congregation located in Tanah Merah, Queensland. The church serves as a dedicated place of worship and community gathering for the Filipino-Australian community in the Logan area. Members gather regularly to participate in worship services and community activities, fostering strong spiritual growth and fellowship.

The congregation holds its main worship services on Thursdays at 7:30 PM and on Sundays at 8:00 AM and 10:30 AM, with the latter accommodating children's worship services. The local chapel provides a warm and welcoming space for families and individuals to gather. Located in the Colonial Square Shopping Centre on Grevillea Street, the venue is easily accessible with ample parking for attendees.

Whether you are a resident in Logan or visiting the Queensland district, the church offers a central hub for community connection and spiritual support. The local administration handles inquiries directly to assist members and visitors with service times or pastoral care. You can reach the congregation at 0422 988 128 or check the official church directory for updates.

name: J & V Bayanihan Filipino Grocery
description:
J & V Bayanihan Filipino Grocery is a well-loved, family-run Asian store located on Lilac Street in Inala, Brisbane. For years, the shop has been a key hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, specialising in hard-to-find Pinoy ingredients and native delicacies.

Locals frequent the shop to satisfy their cravings for authentic treats, noting the impressive variety of ube options and delicious homemade atsara. Visitors also appreciate the fresh bakery items and traditional snacks (merienda) on offer. A major highlight is the friendly and helpful service provided by the owners, Virgie and Josie, who are known to go out of their way to source specific items for customers when stocks are low.

While some shoppers find the prices slightly higher than standard supermarkets, most agree that the convenience and hard-to-find product range make it well worth the visit. Whether you are looking for specific Filipino spicy nuts, frozen specialty meats, or pantry staples to recreate your favourite dishes at home, this welcoming store is a fantastic local go-to spot in Inala.

name: JADE ENTERPRISE
description:
Jade Enterprise is a dedicated courier and shipping service based in Oxley, Queensland, specialising in helping the Filipino-Australian community send goods to the Philippines. As an authorised partner of LBC Australia, this family-managed business provides a reliable connection for sending Balikbayan boxes, sea freight, and air freight directly from Brisbane to various destinations across the Philippines.

Customers frequently praise the business for its exceptionally kind, helpful, and friendly customer service. Managed by Jojo Indopia, Jade Enterprise offers a highly convenient door-to-door box collection service. To ensure a smooth process, collections and deliveries are scheduled during weekends, while the official cargo cutoff takes place every Monday. Residents can easily book their weekend collections by sending a text message with their address and box details.

In addition to serving the immediate Brisbane metropolitan area, the service extends its reach to regional Queensland, including Toowoomba, Dalby, Roma, Chinchilla, Warwick, and Killarney. Whether you are sending a standard large box filled with gifts for your family or coordinating air freight for urgent items, Jade Enterprise ensures your shipments are handled with care and arrive safely at their destination.

name: JD Pinoy & Asian Grocery
description:
JD Pinoy & Asian Grocery is a spacious Filipino and Asian supermarket located in Deception Bay, Queensland. As one of the largest Pinoy specialty stores in the region, it specialises in bringing a taste of home to the local community. Visitors will find a clean, well-organised store stocking over three thousand products, including classic pantry staples, popular snacks, sauces, and frozen goods.

The store features a popular selection of fresh kakanin and Filipino bakery products arriving every Saturday. Customers can also find fresh home-grown vegetables, traditional household goods, and imported Filipino alcohol brands like Red Horse and San Miguel. Beyond groceries, the business provides convenient community services, including Balikbayan cargo box shipping and Ria money transfer.

Local shoppers appreciate the warm, welcoming atmosphere created by the owners, Jane and David, and their helpful team. The store also operates an efficient customer loyalty program that makes shopping here even more rewarding. Convenient on-site parking is available for visitors, and a small Filipino dining venue sits right next door, making it a great destination for a weekend visit.

name: JQ PINOY AUTOMOTIVE
description:
JQ Pinoy Automotive is a trusted Filipino-owned mechanical repair and chauffeur service provider based in Waterford West, Brisbane. Led by June and his experienced team, the business specialises in comprehensive auto repairs, including professional transmission services and general mechanical maintenance.
