They also offer reliable chauffeur and transport transfer services for clients across the local Brisbane community.

Local customers highly recommend JQ Pinoy Automotive for their professional approach and excellent standard of work. The team is known for providing fair, transparent pricing and friendly service, ensuring that every vehicle receives the highest quality care. Whether you need a standard car service, complex transmission repairs, or a dependable transport booking, June and his team deliver efficient and customer-oriented solutions.

The workshop operates seven days a week to accommodate busy schedules. You can visit them or book a service from Monday to Friday between 8:30 am and 6:00 pm, or on weekends from 8:00 am to 4:00 pm. For bookings or general inquiries, contact the team directly on 0450 800 062.

name: Jesus is Lord Church Brisbane
description:
Jesus is Lord Church (JIL) Brisbane is a welcoming Christian fellowship serving the Filipino-Australian community in Kallangur and the surrounding northern suburbs of Brisbane. The church focuses on spiritual growth and community support. Members appreciate the warm, encouraging atmosphere and the strong sense of fellowship. 

The congregation hosts weekly Worship and Healing Services on Sundays at 3:00 PM at 19 Marsden Road, Kallangur. The church offers several regular ministries to support members of all ages. This includes the Christian Youth Network (CYN), which organises fortnightly youth connections on Fridays at 6:00 PM. Members also gather for mid-week Prayer Nights on Tuesdays at 7:30 PM, and a monthly Dawn Watch Prayer on the first Saturday of each month at 5:00 AM. 

To foster closer relationships, JIL Brisbane organises small fellowship groups, or Life Groups, across Bracken Ridge and Kallangur. These groups provide cultural connection and spiritual support for local families. Whether you are looking for a place of worship or a supportive community, JIL Brisbane offers a welcoming environment for everyone.

name: KALYE by The Filipino Street Food
description:
KALYE by The Filipino Street Food is a specialised takeaway eatery situated at 681 Deception Bay Road in Deception Bay, Queensland. As a key part of the broader 'Filipino Street Food' brand, the establishment focuses on delivering a rich variety of traditional and authentic Filipino dishes directly to the local community in the Moreton Bay region. 

Diners frequently visit the venue to enjoy classic comfort food selections. Customer reviews regularly highlight the pork sisig and crispy fried chicken as flavourful favourites that capture authentic home-style cooking. For dessert lovers, the sweet and refreshing halo-halo is also highly recommended. Although some patrons have experienced service delays or food shortages during large, busy outdoor community gatherings, the restaurant remains a recognised and appreciated option for classic Filipino takeaway in the area. 

While their official website offers online ordering, tray orders, and menus for their sister location, Kanto, in nearby Redcliffe, this Deception Bay venue is currently temporarily closed.

name: KANTO by The Filipino Street Food
description: KANTO by The Filipino Street Food is a welcoming, casual restaurant located in Redcliffe, Queensland. Specialising in authentic Filipino flavours, this local favourite serves up a popular range of street food classics and traditional dishes. Visitors frequently praise the cosy, family-friendly atmosphere and the warm hospitality of the owner and staff. The menu features well-loved options such as chicken inasal, crispy pork belly kare-kare, and pork adobo, alongside sweet treats like bibingka and shaved ice halo-halo. Guests also enjoy their high-quality coffee and refreshing smoothies. Many reviews highlight the generous portion sizes, reasonable prices, and the beautifully presented meals. On Saturday nights, the eatery often hosts live acoustic music, creating a lively community vibe. Open from 6:30 am to 2:30 pm Monday to Friday, and 8:30 am to 3:00 pm on Saturdays, it is a perfect spot for brunch or lunch. Whether you are a local resident or a visitor stopping by after a trip to the Sunshine Coast, KANTO offers a wonderful opportunity to experience genuine Filipino hospitality and cuisine in a relaxed suburban setting.

name: KaBarrio Feast
description:
KaBarrio Feast is a welcoming Filipino restaurant located inside the lobby of the Springfield Lakes Hotel. Operated by owners Jon and Allora, this family-friendly venue has become a favourite local spot for authentic Filipino comfort food in a relaxed lakeside setting.

The dining experience is highly regarded for its warm hospitality, with attentive staff who make guests feel right at home. Patrons appreciate the generous portions and budget-friendly prices, which make it an excellent choice for families. The menu highlights a variety of traditional dishes, including flavourful pork sisig, crispy pata, balbacua, beef pares, and classic kare-kare. Other popular selections include crispy lechon, pancit, and family favourites like fried chicken with chips. For dessert, visitors can enjoy refreshing halo-halo, taho, or a sweet leche flan.

In addition to standard dine-in service, KaBarrio Feast specialises in large-group dining, offering a popular Friday and Saturday night eat-all-you-can dinner buffet complete with karaoke. The restaurant also serves the wider community by providing custom party trays and professional catering packages for special celebrations, graduations, and private functions.

name: Kabayan Filipino Store Acacia Ridge
description:
Kabayan Filipino Store in Acacia Ridge is a beloved family-owned grocery hub located in the Acacia Marketplace Shopping Centre. Serving the local Brisbane community, this store specialises in a wide variety of traditional Filipino ingredients, pantry staples, and frozen products, from suman and kikiam to frozen tilapia.

Customers frequently praise the warm, friendly service provided by the owners and staff, who go out of their way to make visitors feel at home. Beyond packaged goods, the store is well-known for its fresh bread deliveries, including favourites like pandesal and ensaymada. On selected days, they also serve delicious prepared Filipino meals such as palabok, beef kaldereta, menudo, and embutido, alongside sweet treats like biko and halo-halo.

In addition to food and grocery items, Kabayan Filipino Store assists the community by offering essential services like money transfer and balikbayan box shipping to send items back home. The store features a neat, well-stocked layout and benefits from the ample parking spaces available within the shopping centre, making it a convenient destination for all your Filipino grocery needs.

name: Kabayan Filipino Store Browns Plains
description:
Kabayan Filipino Store Browns Plains is a friendly, family-owned Asian grocery shop serving the local community in Browns Plains, Queensland. Located within the Andersens Browns Plains complex, this well-organised store specialises in imported goods and traditional pantry staples from the Philippines.

Locals highly appreciate the shop for its neat layout and polite, welcoming customer service. The shelves are stocked with a wide variety of favourite Filipino ingredients, seasonings, household goods, and traditional snacks. A major highlight is the regular supply of fresh bread and kakanin sourced from local bakers. The store also caters to practical community needs by supplying balikbayan boxes and offering Ria Money Transfer services.

Whether you are looking for specific cooking ingredients to prepare classic home-style dishes or searching for traditional snacks and wellness products like effacasent oil, this shop provides a convenient local hub. It is open seven days a week from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, offering reliable service and reasonable prices for the Filipino diaspora and food enthusiasts in the Logan area.

name: Kabayan Filipino Store Chermside
description:
Kabayan Filipino Store Chermside is a spacious Asian and Filipino grocery store situated on Gympie Road in Chermside, Brisbane. Known for its clean layout and wide aisles, the store provides local shoppers with a convenient spot to find authentic Filipino ingredients, snacks, beverages, and traditional baked goods like pandesal. Customers appreciate the clean shopping environment and the helpful service from the on-floor staff. For convenience, dedicated parking is available at the side of the building, making it easier for visitors to load up on groceries.

While the store offers a broad range of Pinoy favourites, stock levels can vary, and customers often advise checking ahead for specific essential items. Although the store has a listed telephone number, response times can sometimes be slow, so planning ahead is recommended.

Whether you are looking for specific pantry staples to cook classic dishes at home or simply craving sweet Filipino treats, this store serves as a key local hub for the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane us northern suburbs.

name: Kabayan Filipino Store Logan Central
description:
Kabayan Filipino Store Logan Central is a welcoming, family-friendly grocery shop located in the Logan City Centre on Wembley Road, Logan Central. Serving the local Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane, this retail store specialises in importing well-known brands and pantry essentials direct from the Philippines.

Inside the clean and well-organised shop, customers will find a comprehensive range of ingredients, from long-life pantry staples and traditional condiments to frozen meats and seafood. Popular snacks, sweets, and personal care items are also regularly stocked, making it a convenient local centre for those seeking home-style goods. A standout favourite among regular visitors is the store's delicious homemade bread pudding.

Community members frequently praise the shop for its reasonable pricing and neat, immaculate shelves. The staff are widely recognised for their polite, helpful, and friendly assistance on the shop floor. Whether you are looking for specific ingredients for an authentic home-cooked meal or just want to pick up some classic Filipino treats, this store provides a reliable and warm shopping experience in the heart of Logan City.

name: Kabayan Filipino Store Redbank Plains
description:
Kabayan Filipino Store Redbank Plains is a local convenience and grocery shop situated in Redbank Plains, Queensland. Conveniently located right next to Grubbies, this store serves as a welcoming hub for the local Filipino-Australian community and anyone keen to explore Filipino flavours.

The shop offers a good selection of imported goods from the Philippines. Customers can find popular snacks, cooking ingredients, condiments like soy sauce, and frozen items. There is also a variety of fresh baked goods, bread, and speciality treats such as chicharon bulaklak.

Locals appreciate having a dedicated grocery store close to home, frequently noting the friendly customer service and the convenience of the location. While the store has received praise for its extensive range of pantry staples and ingredients, some visitors have experienced issues with the freshness of pre-ordered cooked items, such as lechon, and how the business handles feedback.

The store is open every day from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, making it easy to pop in for daily essentials or a weekend treat. Whether you are looking for your favourite Boy Bawang snacks or ingredients for a traditional home-cooked meal, it provides a handy spot to shop.

name: Kabayan Filipino Store Wishart
description:
Kabayan Filipino Store Wishart is a welcoming Asian grocery store situated in Shop 11 of the Civic Fair Shopping Centre on Newnham Road, Wishart. The shop specialises in imported Filipino foods and ingredients, bringing a comforting taste of home to the local Brisbane community.

Inside the well-organised shop, visitors will find a wide variety of Pinoy essentials. The shelves are stocked with a diverse range of snacks, chips, biscuits, and baking ingredients, including popular items like ube flavouring and food dye. Customers highly appreciate the comprehensive selection of frozen meats, fish, cassava, and coconut. The store also offers a rotating range of traditional ice cream flavours.

Locals value the store for its competitive and reasonable pricing. The friendly staff create a warm, welcoming community atmosphere, always greeting shoppers with a smile. Whether you are looking for everyday pantry items, specific seasonings, or ingredients to prepare a special family feast, this store serves as a reliable hub. It provides an authentic and convenient shopping experience for the Filipino-Australian community and anyone keen to explore traditional flavours.

name: Kainan sa Valley
description:
Kainan sa Valley is a cafeteria-style Filipino eatery situated in the McWhirters Centre food court in Fortitude Valley, Brisbane. Located on Floor 0, this convenient spot is positioned directly across from the Fortitude Valley train station. The eatery operates as a popular offshoot of the neighbouring Best Friends Philippine & Asian Grocery Store.

Specialising in traditional 'turo-turo' (point-point) service, the counter features a rotating selection of ready-to-eat comfort food. Customers can choose from classic dishes such as adobo, dinuguan, pancit, and a highly recommended beef caldereta. The restaurant also serves authentic Filipino desserts, including a rich, sweet cassava cake and crispy turon coated in a glassy caramel shell.

With its affordable prices and quick service, Kainan sa Valley provides a welcoming taste of home for the local Brisbane Filipino community. It is an excellent stop for a casual lunch or a quick takeaway meal while passing through the Valley.

name: Karinderia by The Filipino Street Food
description:
Karinderia by The Filipino Street Food is a lively food stall at the Eat Street Northshore markets in Hamilton, Brisbane. This casual market vendor specialises in bringing the vibrant flavours of traditional Filipino street food to Brisbane market-goers.

The stall is best known for its authentic, charcoal-grilled skewers. The pork and chicken BBQ options are popular choices. Another customer favourite is the classic fish balls, which are served with a sweet-savoury dipping sauce. To complement the hot dishes, diners recommend trying the refreshing gulaman, a traditional sweet drink.

Focusing on simple quick bites, the stall provides a unique taste of the Philippines in a high-energy outdoor setting. It operates on weekend market nights from Friday to Sunday. This makes it a great spot for sharing food with friends. Diners can enjoy their meals directly at the communal market seating area. Visitors should note that rice is ordered separately, and the stall can become very busy during peak hours.

name: Karinderia by The Filipino Street Food Hamilton
description: Karinderia by The Filipino Street Food is a popular food stall located in the lively Eat Street Northshore market at Hamilton, Brisbane. Specialising in authentic Philippine street food, this casual weekend eatery brings the vibrant flavours of Manila to local food lovers. Visitors can enjoy classic street snacks like charcoal-grilled pork BBQ skewers, steamed dim sim, and spring rolls. Their popular fish balls are served with a signature sweet and spicy dipping sauce. To accompany the savoury dishes, the stall serves refreshing sago't gulaman, a classic Filipino brown sugar drink. Many diners appreciate the friendly customer service, quick prep times, and charcoal-grilled flavours. Sizable rice meals are available for those seeking a more filling dinner option. The stall gets busy later in the evening, making it a great spot for a quick bite with friends. As part of a local collective that also operates Kanto in Redcliffe, Karinderia provides a welcoming introduction to classic Filipino flavours in a bustling outdoor market setting.

name: Kubo’s Bar & Grill Coorparoo
description:
Kubo’s Bar & Grill Coorparoo was a popular, easygoing restaurant located at Coorparoo Square in Brisbane. The venue specialised in serving traditional Filipino recipes and flavours to the local community. Customers loved the friendly, casual atmosphere and the generous portion sizes of their dishes. It was a lively hub that occasionally hosted live serenades, open mic sessions, and cultural celebrations, making it a favourite gathering place for the Filipino-Australian community.

Among the preferred menu offerings were classic Filipino dishes such as pork adobo, crispy pata, kare-kare, and dinuguan. The restaurant was also known for its pork barbecue skewers, bulalo, Bicol express, and fresh fish ceviche (kinilaw). Guests looking for sweet treats could indulge in traditional Filipino desserts like halo-halo and cassava cake. While some diners noted that pricing was on the medium-to-high side and service could occasionally be slow during peak hours, most agreed the authentic flavours and large portions were highly satisfying. With its central location close to the Brisbane CBD and convenient basement parking options, it served as a welcoming space for families and big groups.

Please note that this business has permanently closed.

name: La Republika Tapas + Bar
description: La Republika Tapas + Bar in Kangaroo Point, Brisbane, was a beloved dining venue that specialised in a unique fusion of authentic Filipino and Spanish cuisines. Designed around sharing plates, the menu brought together vibrant flavours from both cultures, featuring popular dishes like sizzling pork sisig tacos, slow-cooked beef kalderata (tomato and beef stew), and beef kare kare croquettes. Diners could also enjoy classic Filipino desserts, including refreshing halo-halo, jackfruit cassava cake, and ube flan cake, alongside a curated selection of Spanish sangria and Filipino-inspired cocktails such as the Calamansi Margarita and Filipino Sling. Located at 18a Thorn Street near Thorn Street Park and Ride, the venue offered a warm, welcoming atmosphere with friendly service and free on-street parking for guests. The space was also home to community inclusion programs, hosting social events like speed dating for the Kindred Hope Club to support locals with disabilities. Although it was a popular local destination for fusion dining, the business has permanently closed.

name: Lakay Pinoy Mechanic
description:
Lakay Pinoy Mechanic is a trusted, Filipino-owned automotive workshop and mobile service located in Beenleigh, Brisbane. They specialise in diesel fitting, general auto repairs, vehicle maintenance, and auto electrical services. Whether you need a major service or custom auto electrical work, this local workshop provides reliable assistance to keep your vehicle running smoothly.

Local customers highly recommend this service for its capably handled, top-notch servicing. The workshop has earned praise for providing helpful guidance and a stress-free experience from start to finish. Clients appreciate the affordable and competitive pricing, making it a budget-friendly option for quality car care in the community. 

Located at 18 Spanns Rd, Beenleigh, the workshop is open Monday to Saturday from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm, and is closed on Sundays. They are dedicated to supporting the local Filipino and wider Brisbane community with friendly, expert automotive services.

name: Lc Filipino Grocery
description:
Lc Filipino Grocery was a local retail store situated in the suburb of Wacol, within the greater Brisbane metropolitan area of Queensland. The shop specifically catered to the needs of the Filipino-Australian community, offering access to imported products, traditional cooking ingredients, and nostalgic snacks from the Philippines. Local grocery shops of this kind play an important role in helping the diaspora stay connected to their culinary heritage by stocking essential pantry items, such as soy sauces, vinegar, soup mixes, sweet treats, and specialised frozen goods.

Conveniently located for residents in Wacol and the neighbouring south-western suburbs of Brisbane, the shop served as a helpful local hub for those seeking authentic items for home-cooked Filipino dishes like adobo, sinigang, or pancit. It provided a welcoming touch of home for the community, making it much easier to source specific products that are not typically available in mainstream supermarkets.

Although it previously served as a popular resource for local residents, this business has permanently closed.

name: Leo Briones Automotive Services
description: Leo Briones Automotive Services is a registered, Filipino-owned mechanical workshop located in Brendale, in the northern suburbs of Brisbane, Queensland. Dedicated to keeping local drivers safe on the road, the workshop specialises in a comprehensive range of automotive solutions, including car repairs, general auto maintenance, and vehicle air conditioning services. Whether it is a routine logbook service, mechanical repairs, or diagnostic troubleshooting, the workshop is equipped to handle various vehicle makes and models with professionalism and care. Operating under the corporate entity Leo Briones Automotive Pty Ltd, the workshop has established itself as a reliable service provider for the local community in Brendale and the surrounding Moreton Bay region. By focusing on honest advice, quality workmanship, and friendly customer service, they ensure that every vehicle receives the highest standard of attention. Local residents seeking trustworthy mechanical repairs and maintenance can count on their expertise and dedication to get their cars running smoothly.

name: Little Black Pug
description:
Little Black Pug is a welcoming, dog-friendly café situated in the heart of Mount Gravatt. Renowned for its friendly atmosphere and exceptional customer service, this contemporary eatery is a local favourite for breakfast and brunch. The team is led by co-owner Rejoice, who infuses the creative menu with delicious Filipino-inspired flavours drawing from her heritage.

Visitors frequently praise the attentive, caring staff and the warm hospitality. The café is particularly famous for being incredibly pet-friendly. Four-legged friends are treated to puppycinos and house-made puppy meatballs while owners relax in the light-filled back garden patio, situated away from traffic.

For humans, the kitchen specialises in a diverse menu featuring standout dishes like the popular Nacho Imposter, fluffy pancakes topped with fresh fruit, and the classic Benny. The café is highly regarded for its inclusive menu, offering excellent gluten-free, dairy-free, and vegan options. The staff are highly knowledgeable about dietary requirements, making it a safe and popular choice for coeliac diners. To accompany the meals, they serve high-quality specialty coffee and refreshing smoothies. It is a fantastic local gem for catch-ups with friends and pets alike.

name: Lola's Coffee Bar
description:
Lola's Coffee Bar is a cozy, aesthetic cafe located in Tarragindi, Brisbane, sharing a unique space with a local barbershop. Inspired by two grandmothers, Lola Tessie from Bulacan and Lola Flora from Pampanga, the cafe specialises in traditional and unique Filipino flavours.

Customers highly recommend the cafe's signature drinks, particularly the vibrant iced strawberry matcha, the rich dirty iced ube latte, and the creamy ube shake. The menu also features popular pandan matcha and mango matcha lattes, made to order by a friendly and welcoming team.

For food, the cafe serves a variety of Filipino-inspired treats. Visitors love the soft and flavourful ube, pandan, and mango cake slices, alongside seasonal specials such as ube cheesecake pudding. Savory options are also available, including well-seasoned beef empanadas, siopaos, and longganisa rolls.

With limited indoor and outdoor seating, the cafe has a warm, relaxed atmosphere that is perfect for takeaways or quick catch-ups. Located on Ekibin Road East, it is a favourite neighbourhood destination for anyone seeking high-quality coffee and authentic Filipino desserts in Brisbane.

name: Lola's Coffee Bar Kelvin Grove
description: Lola's Coffee Bar Kelvin Grove is a welcoming, Filipino-inspired café situated within the busy QUT precinct in Kelvin Grove, Brisbane. As the brand's second Brisbane location, this cozy venue has quickly become a favourite neighbourhood spot for students, residents, and coffee enthusiasts. The café specialises in creative and vibrant matcha beverages, featuring highly popular choices like the strawberry matcha, ube matcha, and pistachio matcha lattes, as well as their unique cinnamon-infused banana bread matcha. Regulars appreciate that they use authentic, high-quality matcha ingredients. Coffee lovers can also enjoy excellent espresso drinks brewed with beans from local roaster Fonzie Abbott. Alongside their extensive drink menu, Lola's offers a delicious selection of sweet pastries and savoury snacks that highlight traditional home-cooked flavours. Visitors highly recommend the handcrafted empanadas, siopao, pandan croissants, and ube leche flan, alongside hearty breakfast plates like chosilog. The café is celebrated for its cute, homey interior design and pet-friendly outdoor seating area. Most of all, guests praise the exceptionally warm hospitality and friendly service from the staff, who consistently greet customers with bright smiles. Lola's Coffee Bar Kelvin Grove provides a wonderful, relaxed setting to experience a modern twist on classic Filipino tastes.

name: Lumpia Lane Australia
description:
Lumpia Lane Australia is a welcoming, family-owned mobile kitchen that brings authentic Filipino flavours to the Brisbane and Moreton Bay regions, with regular appearances in suburbs like Morayfield, Petrie, and Burpengary. Operating as a mobile food truck and caterer, this local favourite specialises in preparing traditional Filipino comfort food from scratch.

Locals highly recommend the kitchen for its exceptionally friendly service and high-quality dishes. Customers frequently rave about the signature loaded lumpia (crispy spring rolls), which serve as a perfect introduction to Filipino cuisine. Other popular menu highlights include savoury pork siomai, traditional pancit noodles, and rich pork adobo bowls.

In addition to their mobile food truck service, Lumpia Lane Australia caters for private events and parties. They prepare custom party trays of their popular dishes for pickup or delivery within the local area, making them a fantastic choice for community gatherings and special celebrations. You can easily reach out to the family by phone or email to discuss catering options or locate their next trading spot.

name: MCGI Brisbane Chapter
description:
MCGI Brisbane Chapter, representing the Members Church of God International, is a welcoming Filipino Christian congregation located in Coopers Plains, Queensland. The church serves as a spiritual sanctuary and safe haven for local families and individuals looking to connect with a supportive community. Gatherings focus on studying the Bible, prayer, and providing practical service to the wider community.

Worship services and prayer meetings are held regularly throughout the week, with primary gatherings on Wednesday and Saturday evenings. Attendees frequently highlight the warm hospitality of the members, which often includes shared meals and supportive fellowship after services. The congregation places a strong emphasis on grounding all spiritual teachings directly in the biblical scriptures, allowing attendees to seek spiritual guidance in an open, friendly setting. In addition to regular worship, the community participates in various outreach programs, charitable activities, and community care services across the Brisbane region. Visitors from all backgrounds are welcome to attend the services, join the studies, and experience the congregation's supportive environment.

name: Mallory's Jerky
description:
Mallory's Jerky is an award-winning, family-owned Queensland business that specialise in producing premium, handcrafted gourmet jerky. Inspired by traditional Filipino tocino curing recipes from Cebu, they prepare their unique small-batch treats with natural ingredients. The product line includes an exciting variety of beef, chicken, pork, and kangaroo jerky.

Local customers highly recommend their unique range, often praising the tender texture and rich flavours. Favourites include the garlic beef, pork peri-peri, and the highly popular chicken adobo jerky. Many clients enjoy these snacks during road trips or travel, noting they are easy to pack and keep well.

While their main production facility is in Narangba, Mallory's Jerky is well known at local gatherings. They frequently showcase their range at popular community events, including the Eumundi and Kallangur night markets. Customers regularly highlight the warm, friendly service received at these stalls and the speed of their online order deliveries. Whether you are looking for a high-protein snack for a trip or a unique local treat, their handcrafted products offer a delicious taste of local craftsmanship.

name: Manawari Filipino and Asian Foods Distributor Queensland
description:
Manawari Filipino and Asian Foods Distributor is a key wholesale supplier and importer in Hillcrest, Brisbane, specialising in authentic Filipino groceries and goods. They distribute a wide range of popular food and beverage brands directly imported from the Philippines to local retail stores, Asian grocers, restaurants, and families looking for bulk products across Queensland. 

People who want to cook classic dishes at home or stock their shops can find essential ingredients like Maggi Magic Sarap, Frozen Steamed Saba Banana, and Super Tender Juicy Hotdogs in various pack sizes. The business supports the local Filipino-Australian community by ensuring that favourite pantry staples and comfort foods remain highly accessible. 

Operating from Monday to Friday between 9 am and 4 pm, they provide friendly customer service and offer convenient options for both product delivery and on-site warehouse collection. Their main warehouse is located at Unit 9, 15 Corporate Place, Hillcrest, serving as a reliable sourcing hub in the Brisbane metropolitan area.

name: Manila Grill Brisbane
description: Manila Grill Brisbane is a welcoming, family-owned restaurant specialising in authentic Filipino comfort food in Deagon. Located close to the train station with free onsite parking, this casual eatery is a popular spot for locals on Brisbane's northside. The menu features traditional dishes, with highlights including sizzling pork sisig, crispy pata, and a comforting beef sinigang. Customers also love their grilled pork belly and skewers, alongside street food favourites like kwek-kwek, fish balls, and their signature Ilocos empanadas. For a sweet finish, the classic halo-halo and refreshing sago't gulaman are highly recommended. The restaurant offers a homely atmosphere with friendly service, making it a great place to explore Filipino flavours. Because the space is cosy and popular, booking a table in advance is a good idea to avoid longer wait times during busy dinner service. Manila Grill Brisbane serves lunch and dinner on weekends, and opens for dinner from Tuesday to Friday. Dine-in, takeaway, and home delivery are all available.

name: Manyaman Browns Plains
description:
Manyaman is a welcoming, family-owned Filipino restaurant located at Shop 7, 111-121 Grand Plaza Drive in Browns Plains, Brisbane. The eatery specialises in authentic Kapampangan comfort food, bringing the rich culinary traditions of Pampanga directly to the local Queensland community.

Locals highly recommend the restaurant for its generous portions, excellent value, and friendly service. The menu features a delicious range of traditional Filipino favourites, with the crispy pata and pork sisig being particular highlights for their perfect crunch. Diners also love the creamy and exquisite kare-kare, crispy lechon kawali, and flavourful bistek. To round off the dining experience, the dessert selection includes a popular halo-halo topped with delicious ube ice cream, alongside classic treats like leche flan, taho, and refreshing sago gulaman.

With ample parking available in the free lot near Woolworths, Manyaman offers a convenient and vibrant dining atmosphere that feels like a Barrio fiesta. Whether you are popping in for a quick lunch, taking away a hearty dinner, or dining in the cosy booth seating with family, it is a favourite destination for authentic Filipino flavours in Brisbane.

name: Maria's Kitchen Brisbane
description:
Maria's Kitchen Brisbane is a home-based specialty kitchen and bakery located in North Lakes, Queensland. It specialises in freshly prepared, made-to-order Filipino classics and traditional desserts. The kitchen is highly regarded for its authentic flavours, offering a modern twist on beloved dishes.

Customers frequently praise the consistent quality of the food and the exceptionally fast, friendly service. Popular menu items include banana walnut leche flan cake, cassava macapuno cake, beef kaldereta, and special adobo. Their selection of egg pies and double-stuffed premium hopia are outstanding favourites that are perfect for families.

This home-based business operates strictly on a pre-order basis, requiring three to five days' notice. Customers can pick up their orders from North Lakes, located near Costco, with a minimum order of fifty dollars. Delivery is also available across the Brisbane Metropolitan area for orders of eighty dollars or more.

Please note that the kitchen is currently temporarily closed. When open, it provides a convenient way for the local community to enjoy premium, homemade Filipino delicacies for special gatherings or everyday meal preparation.

name: Members Church of God International Australia
description:
Members Church of God International (MCGI) Australia is a welcoming Christian congregation situated in Coopers Plains, Brisbane. The church is dedicated to fostering a warm, supportive, and family-friendly environment, serving as a spiritual haven for local worshippers and their families.

Members of the congregation highly appreciate the church's focus on biblical truth, with services and teachings grounded directly in the scriptures. The community is known for its hospitality, with members looking after visitors and offering free meals during gatherings. Worshippers also enjoy modern conveniences, including a comfortable, fully air-conditioned venue. A unique aspect of their ministry is the Worldwide Bible Exposition, where anyone is welcome to ask questions about the Christian faith and receive answers directly from the Bible, led by their preachers.

Regular worship services are held on Wednesdays at 7:30 pm and Saturdays at 4:30 pm at their Boyland Avenue chapel. Whether you are seeking spiritual guidance, looking to attend a Bible study, or hoping to connect with a caring Christian community, the Brisbane chapter provides a supportive environment for everyone.

name: Mestiza Filipina
description:
Mestiza Filipina is a Brisbane-based Filipino-Australian fashion boutique specialising in modern Filipiniana attire and traditional garments. Co-founded by siblings Louisa and Anthony Parkinson alongside their mother Merly—a highly respected cultural dance choreographer in Queensland—the label creatively reimagines heritage garments into contemporary, wearable fashion. Their extensive collection features hand-embroidered organza boleros with iconic butterfly sleeves, modern Barong Tagalogs, midi dresses, elegant alampays, and hand-beaded jewellery.

Designed in Australia and lovingly handmade by local artisans in the Philippines, Mestiza Filipina focuses on premium craftsmanship and authentic storytelling. Their celebrated 'Harana' runway collection, showcased at the Queensland Arts and Fashion Festival, draws inspiration from provincial life and family farms in Mindanao, blending nostalgia with contemporary styles. Customers consistently praise the exceptional quality of the fabrics, beautiful detailing, and prompt service, making their garments a popular choice for weddings, pageants, and cultural celebrations. Operating as an online-only storefront, Mestiza Filipina offers fast express shipping worldwide, enabling the global Filipino diaspora to proudly celebrate their rich heritage in everyday wardrobes.

name: Miguelitos Ice Cream Brisbane Inala
description:
Miguelitos Ice Cream Brisbane was a popular Filipino dessert spot and café situated in the lively Inala Plaza. Dedicated to bringing a comforting taste of home to the local community, this cosy venue specialised in soft-serve ice cream and a delightful range of traditional sweet treats.

Customers particularly loved the vibrant ube and mango graham soft-serve flavours, which they could customise with a variety of delicious toppings. The unique rolled ice cream options and refreshing mango sago were also major favourites among regular visitors. In addition to sweet desserts, the café served popular savoury items like sweet Filipino-style spaghetti and crispy fried chicken, which locals frequently compared to Jollibee. Reviewers consistently highlighted the warm and accommodating customer service from the friendly staff, as well as the excellent value for money and generous portion sizes. It quickly became a favourite gathering centre for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Filipino flavours in the Brisbane region.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Miguelitos Ice Cream Chermside
description:
Miguelitos Ice Cream Chermside is a beloved Filipino dessert kiosk situated at Westfield Chermside, on Level 1 of the food court in Brisbane. The shop specialises in smooth and creamy soft-serve ice cream featuring traditional Filipino flavours. Local customers particularly enjoy the vibrant ube (purple yam) soft serve and the mango ube cup, both praised for their rich, natural fruit taste. Run by welcoming owners Tess and Marcus, the kiosk offers a friendly atmosphere for families looking to enjoy authentic Pinoy desserts. Many visitors highlight the helpful service and find the treats to be great value for money.

The menu includes unique options such as avocado, coconut, pistachio, and a creative Dubai chocolate soft serve. While some visitors have noted that the servings can occasionally be hollow in the middle or that the vanilla flavour is less sweet than expected, the kiosk remains a popular destination for those seeking a true taste of Philippine sweet treats on the north side of Brisbane. This business is currently temporarily closed.

name: Miguelitos Ice Cream Mt Gravatt
description:
Miguelitos Ice Cream is a welcoming dessert kiosk situated in the Westfield Mt Gravatt shopping centre in Brisbane. Specialising in unique Filipino-style soft-serve treats, this popular spot offers a delightful range of flavours, including ube (purple yam), avocado, mango, and pistachio. The brand is particularly well-known for its health-conscious approach, offering desserts with eighty per cent less sugar than standard soft-serve ice cream, alongside vegan-friendly options.

Local visitors frequently visit to enjoy popular combinations like the ube biscoff and avocado loco. Many customers appreciate the generous portion sizes, clean presentation, and friendly staff. Because the ice cream has a lower sugar content, the flavours are pleasantly subtle and not overly sweet, making them a refreshing choice for a warm Brisbane afternoon. The kiosk is located on the first floor of Westfield Mt Gravatt, providing a perfect stop for families and dessert lovers looking to try authentic Filipino flavours while shopping.

name: Mobile Mechanic Brisbane Northside
description:
Mobile Mechanic Brisbane Northside is a highly regarded, family-owned mobile automotive service run by Ray Gallardo, a passionate and friendly mechanic with over 18 years of industry experience. Operating across Brisbane’s northern suburbs, Ray brings a fully equipped workshop directly to your home, office, or roadside, specialising in Honda vehicles and servicing all other makes and models. Customers appreciate his authentic approach, frequently highlighting his clear explanations, honest pricing, and willingness to offer helpful advice over the phone to save them money.

Whether you require a comprehensive logbook service, brake repairs, a pre-purchase vehicle inspection, or emergency roadside assistance, Ray delivers prompt and professional workmanship. The business handles everything from simple battery replacements and cooling system issues to auxiliary belt and starter motor repairs. Every service and part comes with a reassuring 12-month guarantee, giving you complete peace of mind. As a Honda registered specialist and A1 fully qualified technician, Ray ensures your manufacturer's warranty is preserved while keeping you fully informed and in control of any extra repair work.

name: Mueller Community Church - Filipino Ministry
description:
Mueller Community Church in Rothwell is home to a dedicated Filipino Ministry that provides a welcoming space for the Filipino diaspora in North Brisbane and the Redcliffe Peninsula. Located on the Mueller College campus, this vibrant community group gathers regularly for Bible study, scripture-based fellowship, and mutual support.

Attendees value the church's friendly environment, family-oriented programs, and focus on clear, Bible-centred teaching. The ministry helps members connect with others, share lunches, and grow in their faith together. It serves as a supportive home away from home for individuals and families looking to build meaningful relationships within the local community.

Whether you are looking to join a regular Bible study group or connect with fellow believers, this ministry offers a warm and approachable space for everyone. You can easily find the church on Morris Road in Rothwell, with plenty of on-site parking available for visitors. To learn more about meeting times and upcoming activities, interested individuals can reach out to the church office directly.

name: MundiaAsianShop
description:
MundiaAsianShop is a welcoming Asian grocery store situated in Beenleigh, Brisbane. Located next to Aldi, the shop provides a convenient location with ample parking, making it a favourite spot for locals to complete their shopping rounds.

The shop specialises in a diverse range of imported groceries, canned goods, frozen foods, and traditional sauces. Customers particularly love the selection of fresh vegetables and greens that are stocked regularly to ensure quality. A unique highlight of MundiaAsianShop is its special selection of fresh bread sourced from a local Filipino bakery, which attracts many shoppers seeking authentic tastes from home.

Visitors frequently praise the friendly owner and helpful staff who assist customers in finding their cooking essentials. The tidy shelves are neatly arranged, creating a pleasant environment for browsing. Whether you are looking for specific Asian ingredients or grabbing last-minute items for a home-cooked meal, this local shop offers reasonable prices and a warm community atmosphere.

name: Narangba Pinoy Basketball
description:
Narangba Pinoy Basketball is a community-focused sports group located at the Morayfield Sport and Events Centre in Morayfield, Queensland. Operating within the northern region of Brisbane, this organisation serves as a welcoming gathering point for local Filipino-Australian players and their families. The group provides a friendly environment where participants can engage in social games, training sessions, and community tournaments.

By promoting physical fitness and team spirit, Narangba Pinoy Basketball helps foster strong cultural ties. It also builds lasting friendships among members of the local diaspora. Activities are hosted at the modern Morayfield Sport and Events Centre. This venue offers excellent indoor court facilities, ensuring a high-quality sporting experience for players of all skill levels.

While they lack public reviews or active operating hours, the group remains a valued local initiative. It successfully brings the community together through a shared passion for basketball. Families and spectators are always welcome to attend, cheer for their favourite teams, and connect with other community members.

name: Night Shift Ballers
description:
Night Shift Ballers is a registered community basketball league that brings together the Filipino-Australian community in South-East Queensland. As one of the largest all-Filipino sports organisations in the region, they specialise in hosting the highly anticipated Sunday Basketball Cup (SBC). Matches and pre-season tournaments are typically organised at premium indoor venues, including the Logan Metro Sports & Events Centre in Crestmead, Nissan Arena, and the Sleeman Sports Complex.

Members of the local community highly recommend the league for its friendly, well-organised, and competitive environment. Players love the fair matchmaking, well-run scrimmage sessions, and the welcoming atmosphere where participants can focus on staying active and healthy. The league has grown into a vital social centre, fostering lifelong friendships and a strong sense of belonging among basketball enthusiasts.

Under the guidance of a dedicated committee, Night Shift Ballers cooperates with local councils and key community sponsors to offer a professional sports experience complete with media days, team meetings, and exciting grand finals. Whether you are an experienced player looking to test your skills on the court or an individual seeking a supportive fitness group, the Sunday Basketball Cup offers a fantastic space to connect with the local community.

name: Northside Dental Solutions
description:
Northside Dental Solutions is a family-focused dental clinic based in Clayfield, Brisbane, specialising in general, cosmetic, and emergency dental care. The dedicated team includes Dr Yohan Joo, a Filipino-Australian dentist, alongside Dr Priyena, Dr Trent, Dr Cassandra Peters, and Dr Adelaide Smith. The practice is highly regarded by the local community for its gentle, patient-focused approach, making it an excellent choice for individuals who experience dental anxiety. 

Patients frequently praise the team for their clear, thorough explanations and calm, reassuring manner during procedures. Key services include routine check-ups, teeth cleans, fillings, wisdom teeth removals, composite veneers, and dental implants. To make each visit more relaxing, the clinic features screens above the treatment chairs where patients can stream Netflix shows. 

Conveniently located at 10 Vine Street, Northside Dental Solutions offers transparent, competitive pricing and flexible payment options such as DentiCare and no-gap preventative care for eligible health funds. The friendly reception staff and accommodating clinic hours ensure a welcoming, stress-free experience from start to finish.

name: Nundah Seventh-day Adventist Church
description:
Nundah Seventh-day Adventist Church is a warm and welcoming Christian congregation located at the Toombul Shire Hall in Nundah, Brisbane. The church is widely recognised for its strong, active Filipino community fellowship, offering a friendly environment where members and visitors are greeted with smiles.

Services are held on Saturdays, beginning with morning worship and Bible study from 9:30 am. A central highlight of the weekly gathering is the shared potluck lunch every Saturday. This community meal provides a wonderful opportunity for attendees to connect, enjoy traditional dishes, and build lasting friendships. Visitors frequently appreciate the uplifting worship atmosphere, the close-knit bonds among the congregation, and the genuine hospitality.

Whether you are looking to participate in Sabbath services, engage in spiritual study, or connect with a vibrant Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane, this church offers an encouraging space to grow. It acts as both a spiritual home and a community hub where everyone is invited to share in worship and fellowship.

name: Orient Freight Balikbayan Boxes
description:
Orient Freight Balikbayan Boxes offers reliable door-to-door cargo and balikbayan box shipping services from Brisbane and the Gold Coast to the Philippines. Operating in partnership with the Pinoy Warehouse in Arundel, this service has connected families for over twenty-five years. They specialise in sea freight deliveries with standard box sizes, allowing customers to easily send personal items to their loved ones.

Customers appreciate the helpful and polite customer service team, including Denise, Jenice, and Raisa, who assist in English, Tagalog, and Bisaya. Shippers can order empty boxes, track their shipments, and complete customs declaration forms online. Standard box rates vary depending on the destination region in the Philippines, starting from one hundred and forty dollars for Metro Manila up to one hundred and seventy-five dollars for inter-island locations.

While shipping durations can vary, many community members value the trust and convenience of their long-running service. The local depot is open daily from Monday to Friday from nine in the morning until five in the afternoon, with convenient weekend hours on Saturdays and Sundays. This makes it easy for families across Queensland to drop off their packages and send care packages home securely.

name: Our Lady of Graces Catholic Church Carina
description:
Our Lady of Graces Catholic Church in Carina is a vibrant and welcoming parish community in Brisbane. Staffed by the Dominican Friars, this church has served local parishioners for over seven decades. The church is widely known for its peaceful environment and beautiful, clean facilities, including a well-designed interior and a spacious car park that makes visiting easy on weekends.

Visitors frequently praise the friendly and knowledgeable Dominican priests, who provide comforting liturgical services and warm pastoral care. Beyond regular worship, the church is a popular venue for family sacraments such as baptisms and christenings, where community members are made to feel right at home.

The parish is active in social justice and community outreach, maintaining close ties with the local school and supporting charity programs like the Australian Filipino Augustinian Solidarity (AFAS) initiatives. Mass is celebrated daily, with Sunday services at 7:00 am, 9:00 am, and 5:30 pm, alongside a dedicated Italian-language mass at 10:30 am. Reconciliation is offered on Saturdays at 8:30 am and 5:30 pm.

name: Our Lady of The Southern Cross Parish
description:
Our Lady of the Southern Cross Parish is a welcoming Catholic church located in Springfield Lakes. Consecrated in 2017, the church building features modern architecture and is adorned with beautiful, sacred artwork by Marko Rupnik. Under the guidance of Father Mauro, who is highly regarded for his kindness and wisdom, the parish has grown into a friendly, inclusive, and multicultural congregation.

Worshippers appreciate the weekly holy Mass services, which are focused on active community participation and accompanied by uplifting hymn selections. The parish plays an active role in the local community by organising youth programs, sacramental preparation, and connect groups.

For the Filipino-Australian community in Greater Brisbane, the church is a particularly significant cultural and spiritual hub. It hosts the traditional Simbang Gabi, a nine-day Christmas novena of early morning masses held from December 15 to 23 at 5:30 AM, followed by a community breakfast. This event regularly brings people together to celebrate faith and heritage. Whether you are visiting for Sunday worship or joining the seasonal festivities, you will find a warm, supportive community here.

name: PIA Cafe and Restaurant
description:
PIA Cafe and Restaurant was a cozy Filipino and Korean fusion venue situated in the heart of Bulimba. Operating from Oxford Street, it specialised in blending traditional flavours from both cuisines to offer a unique local dining experience.

Diners frequently highlighted the exceptionally friendly service and the generous portion sizes, which made it a popular choice for families and casual diners. The menu featured a range of traditional Filipino breakfast classics, including silog sets like tapsilog and tosilog, alongside sizzling pork sisig. These dishes were served alongside Korean comfort food favourites such as Korean fried chicken, bibimbap, and kimchi soup. At the front of the restaurant, a dedicated section known as the Dal Dal store specialised in preparing sweet corndogs, thick shakes, and bubble tea.

This business has permanently closed its Bulimba location. The owners have transitioned their operations, relocating their dedicated Filipino restaurant, Pia's Kusina, to Warry Street in Fortitude Valley.

name: Pamilya Accountants
description:
Pamilya Accountants is a family-owned and operated tax accounting firm based in Bundall on the Gold Coast, specialising in providing comprehensive financial solutions for individuals and families across South East Queensland. Established and led by Director Michelle Gargar (FCPA), who has been in practice since 1990, the firm focuses on delivering trustworthy, friendly, and personalised services. Their core offerings include tax return preparation, bookkeeping, annual financial reports, business activity statements (BAS), and self-managed super funds (SMSF) management.

Clients highly recommend the team for being exceptionally helpful, honest, and reliable. Michelle is widely appreciated for going above and beyond to understand each client's unique financial situation. She takes the time to ask the right questions and offers clear, valuable guidance to help clients make informed decisions about their personal and business finances.

The office is open from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Friday, and is closed on weekends. With a strong commitment to the local community, they aim to make complex tax and accounting matters simple and accessible for everyone.

name: Perlas Asian Shop
description:
Perlas Asian Shop was a beloved local grocery store situated in Bracken Ridge, Brisbane, that specialised in importing a wide variety of Filipino groceries, pantry staples, treats, and Asian ingredients.

Locals and families frequently visited the shop to stock up on their favourite traditional Filipino pantry items, including rice noodles and unique products that were otherwise difficult to find in the northern suburbs of Brisbane.

Customers warmly remembered the shop for its fair pricing and massive product selection relative to its cosy retail space, often leaving with arms full of wonderful goods.

The staff, particularly the owner Perla (warmly known as Tita Perla), were often described as lovely, friendly, and welcoming, which made every visit a pleasant shopping experience for the local community.

In addition to authentic Filipino products, the shop also stocked a popular range of other Asian items, such as Korean Buldak spicy noodles.

Perlas Asian Shop was located at Unit 4/63 Gawain Road in Bracken Ridge.

Following a closing down sale in July 2022, this business has permanently closed.

name: Philippine Consulate, Queensland
description:
The Philippine Consulate in Queensland is an honorary consular office located in Fortitude Valley, Brisbane. It offers vital administrative services to the local Filipino community, including processing NBI clearances, document notarisation (such as Special Power of Attorney), and handling reports of birth, marriage, and death.

Visitors frequently highlight the efficiency and warmth of the service at this Brisbane office. The dedicated staff and volunteers, including highly commended team members like Gemma, Von, and Joseph, are widely praised for their helpfulness and patient guidance. They assist visitors through form processing and photocopy requirements, ensuring a smooth and straightforward experience. Many appreciate how accommodating the team is, especially when helping families with young children or coordinating double appointment slots. While booking an appointment is required, the consulate is commended for maintaining an on-time and welcoming environment.

Conveniently situated on Wickham Street, the office acts as a central support hub for Queensland-based Filipinos. It regularly shares updates on mobile consular missions organised by the main embassy to handle passport renewals and dual citizenship applications. The consulate helps local residents manage official tasks close to home, reflecting a strong spirit of community care and professional dedication.

name: Philippine Express Freight & Allied Services
description:
Philippine Express Freight & Allied Services is a Filipino-owned cargo and freight forwarding business located in Carole Park, Queensland. The service specialises in assisting the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane with shipping goods and sending balikbayan boxes to the Philippines.

Customers frequently praise the business for its reliable and fast air freight options, which typically deliver parcels within a week. For larger shipments, they also offer dependable sea cargo services. In addition to transport, community members appreciate the convenient money transfer services that provide competitive rates. While the team is highly regarded for its friendly and approachable service, a minor portion of customer feedback highlights occasional delays, which is typical for international logistics.

Conveniently situated in the industrial area of Carole Park, they have built long-term relationships with local clients who have trusted them with their shipments for many years. Whether you are sending a care package to family or transferring funds overseas, this service provides a helpful connection to the Philippines.

name: Philippine Multi-Purpose Centre
description:
The Philippine Multi-Purpose Centre is a vibrant community hub located at Beryl Roberts Park on 45 Barham Street in Coopers Plains, Brisbane. Established in 1996 and operated by the Filipino-Australian Foundation of Queensland Inc. (FAFQ Inc.), the centre acts as a welcoming 'home away from home' for both long-term residents and new arrivals. 

Inside the facility, the community has access to a range of cultural and educational resources, including a dedicated Filipiniana library. FAFQ Inc. runs several structured programs here, such as the Filipino Language School, along with classes in arts, crafts, traditional dance, and music. The centre is also a venue for youth leadership training, professional networking, and charitable initiatives like the annual Charity Queen Australia pageant.

Local community members highly respect the kind-hearted and humble leaders who manage this organisation. Whether you are looking to learn the language, attend cultural celebrations, or connect with support services, this centre offers a supportive space that fosters unity and celebrates Filipino heritage in Queensland.

name: Philippine Travel & Trade
description:
Philippine Travel & Trade is a trusted, independent travel agency based in Riverhills, Brisbane, specialising in personalised travel planning for all types of travellers. With over 29 years of combined industry expertise and recognition as a Certified FunTravel Expert by the Philippine Department of Tourism, the agency is well-equipped to design custom itineraries for domestic and international trips.

Clients highly appreciate the agency's warm and accommodating service, frequently describing the staff as courteous, professional, and helpful. This client-focused approach ensures a stress-free travel planning experience from start to finish.

The agency provides a comprehensive range of travel services. These include airline ticketing, land tour arrangements, and cruise vacations, alongside travel insurance packages to secure your journey. Additionally, they can arrange car rentals, ferry bookings, and train tickets to ensure smooth transport at your destination. Whether you are planning a family holiday, or a visit back to the Philippines, Philippine Travel & Trade offers expert guidance and tailored solutions to make your next adventure seamless and memorable.

name: Philippines-Australia Multicultural Association - PAMA Inc.
description:
The Philippines-Australia Multicultural Association (PAMA) Inc. is a not-for-profit, Filipino-led community organisation based in the Greater Springfield and Ipswich region of Queensland. Established in April 2021, the association fosters cross-cultural friendships, understanding, and integration through community sports, welfare, and cultural festivities. PAMA connects Filipino migrants and settlers with other multicultural groups in Australia, extending support to those experiencing difficulties and advocating for their interests. The organisation is highly active in promoting harmony and cooperation, helping to create a safe, welcoming, and empowering environment for the diverse community.

PAMA is best known for organising PAMA SpringFest, a major annual multicultural festival held at Robelle Domain Parklands in Springfield Central. This flagship event brings together thousands of attendees to celebrate unity in diversity with cultural performances, diverse food stalls, and creative arts. The association actively encourages volunteer participation, collaboration, and sponsorship from local businesses. Led by dedicated committee members, PAMA is committed to preserving cultural heritage and leaving a positive community legacy.

name: Pia's Kusina
description:
Pia's Kusina is a welcoming, family-run Filipino cafe and restaurant located in the heart of Fortitude Valley, Brisbane. Specialising in authentic, home-style Filipino cuisine, this local gem serves a diverse menu of traditional favourites and modern twists.

Diners love their signature all-day breakfast dishes, including flavourful pork tocilog, longsilog, and their unique longganisa-filled breakfast muffin served with chips. Other popular menu items include sizzling pork sisig, kwek kwek appetisers, and creative fusions like creamy adobo pasta. For a refreshing sweet treat, they prepare fresh ube smoothies and traditional buko pandan.

The dining space is clean, cosy, and spacious, featuring high ceilings, bright lighting, and a comfortable outdoor seating area. Guests consistently appreciate the warm, accommodating hospitality from Monique and the team, creating a relaxed atmosphere that feels just like home.

Pia's Kusina is located at 23 Warry Street, Fortitude Valley. They are open Monday to Friday from 6:00 am to 2:00 pm, and Saturdays from 7:00 am to 3:00 pm, making it a perfect spot for early breakfast, brunch, or a hearty lunch with friends and family.

name: Pilipino Australian Social Club
description: The Pilipino Australian Social Club was a community-based organisation located in Kingston, Queensland. It was established to serve and support the Filipino-Australian community within the wider Brisbane and Logan regions. As a dedicated cultural and social hub, the group focused on preserving Filipino heritage and fostering strong community connections among its members. The club organised various social gatherings, cultural celebrations, and support initiatives, helping Filipino migrants adjust to their new life in Australia while maintaining a connection to their cultural roots. The club's activities offered a welcoming environment for families and individuals to socialise, share experiences, and celebrate traditional Filipino festivals and events together. Through these efforts, the group aimed to promote unity, mutual support, and cultural pride within the local area, working in collaboration with other regional organisations. It provided an important point of contact for new arrivals seeking assistance and friendship. However, this organisation is no longer active, and the Pilipino Australian Social Club has permanently closed.

name: Pinay Gifts & Accessories
description:
Pinay Gifts & Accessories was a popular Filipino grocery and gift shop in Upper Mount Gravatt, Brisbane. Conveniently situated in the Palmdale Shopping Centre, the store was located just across from Garden City and offered ample parking. For many years, local residents visited the shop to purchase their favourite Filipino grocery items. The shelves were well-stocked with a wide range of products, including traditional snacks, noodles, canned goods, and frozen specialities like longganisa. Customers could also find daily essentials, such as laundry detergents, shampoos, fresh bread, and delicious pastries.

Beyond groceries, the business functioned as a vital hub for the local Filipino community. It provided a reliable door-to-door parcel delivery service to anywhere in the Philippines. Additionally, it operated as a Ria Money Transfer agent, offering convenient door-to-door money transfer services. Visitors consistently praised the welcoming and friendly environment. The owner, Mariles, was known for her excellent customer service and always ensured that popular items remained available. This business has permanently closed.

name: Pinoy Delights
description:
Pinoy Delights is a casual Filipino restaurant located in the food court of Sunnybank Hills Shoppingtown in Brisbane. The eatery is well-known within the local community for serving authentic, homestyle comfort food that reminds diners of traditional family cooking.

Customers frequently praise the warm hospitality and friendly service provided by the hard-working owners. Notable highlights on the menu include popular silog meals, such as bangsilog, which features crispy boneless milkfish served with fragrant garlic fried rice and eggs. The kitchen also prepares a range of traditional Filipino mains, including pork adobo, beef kare-kare, chicken inasal, and crispy pata. For those seeking snacks or sweet treats, the restaurant offers freshly made empanadas, banana que, turon, cassava cake, and halo-halo.

Whether stopping by for a hearty brunch or picking up a quick takeaway snack, visitors will find a welcoming atmosphere and generous portions. Pinoy Delights operates seven days a week, opening daily at 9:00 am and closing at 5:00 pm, with extended trading hours until 7:30 pm on Thursdays.

name: Pinoy Flava
description:
Pinoy Flava was a welcoming Filipino specialty grocery shop and eatery that operated on Logan Road in Underwood, Brisbane. The business served as an important hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, offering a clean and well-organised space that made shopping easy. Local customers fondly remember the shop for its friendly and helpful customer service, which created a warm and inviting environment.

The shop specialised in a diverse range of imported pantry staples, fresh grocery items, and traditional sweet treats. Visitors particularly enjoyed the selection of hot, freshly cooked savoury Filipino dishes and baked goods. In addition to its retail offerings, the store provided valuable money transfer services, making it convenient for patrons to support their families overseas. While some shoppers noted that the variety of brands and stock levels could be limited at times, the overall consensus was highly positive, highlighting the convenience of having authentic products close to home.

Please note that this business has permanently closed its physical store operations. However, it remains a cherished part of Underwood's culinary memory for those seeking authentic Filipino flavours in the Brisbane region.

name: Pinoy Goodies
description:
Pinoy Goodies is a welcoming Filipino grocery store located in Strathpine, Brisbane, offering local residents a convenient way to access authentic products from the Philippines. The shop has become a popular destination due to its warm, helpful customer service and friendly atmosphere.

Visitors love the extensive range of classic Filipino snacks, grocery items, and specialty goods. The owner, Kim, is widely praised for greeting customers like family and sharing helpful knowledge about the products. The shop is well-presented, clean, and easy to find, with plenty of on-street and off-street parking available.

In addition to the physical storefront, Pinoy Goodies offers online shopping and shipping options across Australia. Customers can easily order their favourite treats through the business's Facebook page, making it a reliable choice for those living further away. The store also coordinates pre-orders for fresh food items, such as traditional lechon baboy. Whether you are searching for pantry essentials or looking to satisfy a nostalgic craving, this local centre provides a reliable connection to Filipino culture and community flavours.

name: Pinoy Grill
description:
Pinoy Grill is a warm and welcoming Filipino restaurant situated in Calamvale's Central Park shopping complex. The eatery specialises in authentic street food and home-style dishes from the Philippines, serving the local Brisbane community. Locals highly recommend the restaurant for its bright atmosphere and rich flavours that capture genuine Filipino hospitality.

Among the crowd-favourite dishes is the crispy pork sisig, which is widely praised for its texture and taste. Visitors also enjoy the perfectly balanced chicken adobo and the rich kare-kare. For street food enthusiasts, the kwek-kwek and refreshing gulaman drink offer a nostalgic taste of the Philippines. Other notable menu items include succulent beef and pork skewers, bicol express, ginataang laing, and hearty kaldereta. Dessert lovers can also indulge in their popular leche flan.

The dining experience is enhanced by the friendly and accommodating service, with the team and owner happy to assist with food intolerances. Whether you choose to dine in or order takeaway, Pinoy Grill provides a comforting taste of home-cooked Filipino cuisine in a relaxed neighbourhood setting.

name: Pinoy Homes Brisbane
description:
Pinoy Homes Brisbane is a dedicated housing consultancy service based in Fortitude Valley, Brisbane. They specialise in assisting the local Filipino-Australian community with the home-buying and building process. The experienced team, including specialists like Adam and Kristine, works closely with premium builder partners and expert finance brokers. They provide end-to-end guidance, helping families secure finance solutions, find the right land, and select home designs tailored to their specific lifestyle needs.

Local clients highly recommend the team for their supportive, friendly, and thorough approach. First-time buyers appreciate the team's patience and clear communication, which helps make the complex process of securing home-and-land packages much less stressful. By providing regular updates and explaining each step clearly, from finance broker coordination to construction handover, they ensure families feel supported throughout. With over 500 families successfully guided into new homes, they have established themselves as a trusted community resource. They operate out of their office in Fortitude Valley and are proud to help families build a secure future in Queensland.

name: Pinoy Services Pty Ltd
description:
Pinoy Services Pty Ltd is a professional, Filipino-owned cleaning and trade services provider. They offer comprehensive cleaning and maintenance solutions for homes and businesses. Servicing Brisbane, the Gold Coast, and Sydney, the company specialises in keeping spaces clean, fresh, and welcoming. Their primary offerings range from general house and commercial cleaning to garden care, lawn mowing, gutter cleaning, pressure washing, and window washing.

Led by CEO Almark Lorenzo, the team works with a personal touch. They focus on the finer details of property maintenance. Customers frequently praise their reliability, trustworthiness, and attention to detail. Reviews highlight the high standard of their work on both routine cleans and tasks like roof and gutter maintenance. The team operates Monday to Friday from 7:00 am to 5:00 pm. They aim to save clients valuable time and deliver peace of mind. Whether you need a regular home spruce-up or professional office care, they ensure every space is left in immaculate condition.

name: Pinoy Warehouse
description:
Pinoy Warehouse is a welcoming family-operated Filipino grocery retailer and wholesaler located in Arundel, QLD. Serving the Gold Coast and Brisbane communities near Harbour Town Shopping Centre, the warehouse offers a massive selection of authentic Pinoy goods.

Visitors will find an impressive range of imported food items, including traditional sauces, noodles, frozen items, and snacks. Local cooks can pick up specialty ingredients like Pagasa malunggay leaves, Sarangani Bay bangus, and Barrio Fiesta shrimp paste. The store also stocks hard-to-find homewares such as puto kutsinta moulders, alongside popular Pinoy beauty products. Beyond groceries, the warehouse serves as a community hub by providing convenient Western Union and RIA money transfer options, as well as Orient Freight Balikbayan box cargo shipping.

Local customers highly recommend the warehouse for its friendly, helpful customer service and welcoming family vibe. As a wholesale distributor, it offers very reasonable prices, making it a budget-friendly option for families. Easy parking on-site makes shopping trips completely stress-free.

name: Pinoy and Asian Eatery
description:
Pinoy and Asian Eatery is an unpretentious, family-run Filipino restaurant and takeaway shop in Deception Bay. The eatery is run by its friendly owner, Tita Rose. She is known for welcoming visitors with warm hospitality.

Local diners highly recommend the restaurant for its authentic, home-style Filipino food and excellent value. Regulars particularly enjoy the breakfast options. These include the classic Aussie breakfast and the Pinoy Big Breakfast, which features garlic rice, beef tapa, longganisa, and tocino. Other popular menu items include crispy fried chicken, grilled liempo, chicken and pork adobo, pork sinigang, and bulalo. For noodle lovers, the menu offers pancit bihon, pancit canton, and sweet-style Filipino spaghetti.

As the owner sometimes manages the eatery alone, service can take a little time when busy. However, customers agree that the fresh, flavourful dishes are well worth the wait. You can pop in for a quick takeaway meal or sit down for a casual lunch. Either way, this cosy spot provides a wonderful taste of traditional Filipino street food in the Moreton Bay Region.

name: QUT Filipino Students Association
description:
The QUT Filipino Students Association (QUTFSA) is a vibrant, student-led organisation at the Queensland University of Technology (QUT) in Brisbane. Recognised as the QUT Guild's 2023 International Club of the Year, the association serves as a welcoming community and support hub for Filipino, Filipino-Australian, and international students interested in Filipino culture.

The association focuses on helping students adjust to university life, fostering camaraderie, and sharing the rich heritage of the Philippines. QUTFSA organises a diverse range of social activities, cultural events, and networking opportunities throughout the academic year. Highlights include Welcome Nights, Pyjama Nights, Karaoke Nights, and the QUTFSA x Lola's Picnic Party. The club also runs creative initiatives like the QUTFSA's Got Talent competition and hosts active communities on their official Discord server and social media platforms.

Membership is open to all students keen to connect, make new friends, and celebrate Filipino culture. The association is based at the QUT Gardens Point Campus and Kelvin Grove Campus in Brisbane City.

name: Queensland Filipino Arts Association Inc. (QFAAA)
description:
The Queensland Filipino-Australian Arts Association Inc. (QFAAA) is a vibrant non-profit organisation based in Brisbane, Queensland. Established in September 2018, the association serves as a supportive platform for Filipino-Australian artists to showcase their talents and share their cultural heritage. Guided by the philosophy 'in diversified arts, we unite', QFAAA works to promote cultural diversity, artistic excellence, and harmony within the multicultural community of Australia.

The association specialises in fostering growth across several artistic categories, including visual arts, literature, performing arts, and applied arts. Community members highly value QFAAA for organising high-standard signature events, such as the annual Dancing Stars Shine talent competition and the TIPON art exhibitions. These projects encourage artistic innovation, mentorship, and collaboration between experienced and aspiring artists. Through these activities, the organisation helps artists integrate their work with other multicultural groups.

Strategic leadership is guided by dedicated founders, including President Gina Luck, who have helped the group expand its presence in Brisbane. QFAAA welcomes individuals who want to become members, sponsor events, or support the local art scene. For updates on upcoming events and projects, check their active Facebook page.

name: Queensland Filipino Youth Association (QFYA)
description:
The Queensland Filipino Youth Association (QFYA) was a community organisation based in Brisbane, Queensland. The group dedicated its efforts to supporting, uniting, and empowering Filipino-Australian youth and international students. By working alongside major state community bodies, the association created a welcoming network for young people adjusting to life in Australia.

QFYA focused on fostering leadership opportunities and encouraging personal growth among its members. The organisation held social gatherings, cultural programs, and professional networking events. These activities celebrated Filipino heritage and helped students overcome challenges like homesickness. In addition, the group represented youth interests in forums with community leaders and government officials. They collaborated with university groups like the QUT Filipino Students Association and FilOz UQ. This cooperation strengthened connections across the wider Filipino diaspora in Queensland.

Members valued the association for providing a sense of family away from home. The group also helped members build confidence and professional skills. Through various collaborative initiatives, the association promoted multiculturalism and positive community relations in the region.

This organisation has permanently closed.

name: Queensland Filipino-Australian Arts Association
description:
The Queensland Filipino-Australian Arts Association (QFAAA) is an active non-profit community group established in September 2018. Operating under the guiding principle of 'in diversified arts, we unite', the association provides a welcoming creative platform to discover, foster, and showcase Filipino-Australian artists across Queensland. QFAAA works to promote cultural diversity and social harmony through a wide array of artistic disciplines, including visual arts, literature, performing arts, and applied arts.

The organisation is well-known in the Brisbane community for curating and hosting key cultural events. These include the 'TIPON' and 'Collective Contemporary Multi Arts' (CCMA) exhibitions, which highlight the unique stories and heritage of local visual artists.
