QFAAA also runs popular talent search programs like the 'Danzing Stars Shine' singing competition to nurture musical talents. 

Led by a dedicated board of directors, including co-founders Gina Luck and Reena Gabutero-Johnson, the association also collaborates with groups such as the Chamber of Commerce and Industry Australia-Philippines (CCIAP). QFAAA aims to encourage mentorship, enable knowledge sharing between experienced and aspiring creatives, and build strong cultural ties between Australia and the Philippines. Aspiring members can connect with the group online to join the community.

name: Radio 4EB Filipino Group
description:
Radio 4EB Filipino Group is a vibrant community radio broadcaster based at Kangaroo Point in Brisbane. As part of South-East Queensland's multilingual broadcasting landscape, this group produces engaging Tagalog-language programs that keep cultural connections alive for the local Filipino-Australian community.

Listeners highly value the group's dedication to celebrating diversity and bringing different ethnicities together. The station is run by passionate volunteers and friendly staff who provide excellent guidance to participants. In addition to general programming, the group offers fantastic workshops and educational activities. These sessions give children practical experience in radio broadcasting, storytelling, and creative collaboration.

The broadcaster produces several weekly shows to keep the community informed and entertained. On Mondays, listeners can tune in to 'Magandang Gabi Brisbane' on 98.1 FM from 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM for local news and immigration updates. This is followed by 'Filipino Jeepney' and 'Filipino LIFE' from 9:00 PM to 10:00 PM on Global Digital Radio. Cultural programs continue on Wednesdays with 'Filipino Mabuhay!', and Thursdays feature the women's empowerment show 'Pinay Chika Tayo', both broadcasting from 9:00 PM to 10:00 PM.

name: Rex's Cuisine
description:
Rex’s Cuisine is a welcoming, family-run Filipino restaurant located in Acacia Ridge, Brisbane. This licensed venue specialises in bringing authentic, soul-warming Pinoy flavours to the local community on the city's southside.

Diners consistently praise the friendly, family-like hospitality and the generous portions of traditional favourites. A major highlight is the signature crispy pata, featuring a deep-fried pork knuckle with golden, crunchy crackling and tender meat served with a house-blended soy dipping sauce. Other popular offerings include rich and tender beef pares with garlic rice, smoky pork skewers, sizzling sisig, dinuguan, and Bicol express. For dessert, customers can enjoy ube yema cake, sapin-sapin, and refreshing halo-halo. The restaurant has also expanded its menu to offer a selection of wines, beers, and soju.

With its relaxed yet polished vibe, the dining room is ideal for casual family dinners, date nights, or group catchups. Additionally, they provide highly professional and informative catering services for local events.

You can find the restaurant at Shop 24/28 Elizabeth Street. While walk-ins are welcome, bookings are highly recommended on weekends due to its popularity. Ample free parking is available on-site.

name: Ria's Pastries & Cakes Geebung
description:
Ria's Pastries & Cakes is a beloved Filipino bakery and café situated at Shop 2, 16 Railway Parade in Geebung, Brisbane. Known warmly to locals as a patisserie home away from home, this charming venue specialises in authentic Filipino baked goods, pastries, and custom celebratory cakes.

The bakery is particularly famous for its spectacular purple ube creations, including ube cupcakes, signature ube drip cakes, and a popular ube cake layered with smooth leche flan. Customers also highlight their traditional ensaymada, buko pies, yema cakes, and savoury steamed pork buns. Every treat is baked to perfection, focusing on balanced flavours that are not overly sweet.

With additional branches in Sunnybank and Acacia Ridge, Ria's has established a strong community presence across Brisbane. The Geebung storefront offers a welcoming atmosphere, reasonable prices, and exceptionally friendly service from a dedicated team. It is a fantastic destination for anyone wishing to experience authentic Filipino flavours or simply grab a delicious sweet treat during their daily commute.

name: Ria’s Pastries and Cakes Sunnybank
description:
Ria’s Pastries and Cakes Sunnybank is a welcoming, family-run Filipino bakery and café on Station Road in Sunnybank. This local favourite specialises in traditional Filipino baked goods. These delicious treats bring a taste of the Philippines to Brisbane.

Customers frequently visit for signature ube cakes, sweet yema cake cups, and freshly baked ensaymadas. These pastries are available in various flavours. Traditional delicacies like savoury siopao buns, buko pie, and egg pie are also popular menu items. For breakfast, the classic pandesal buns pair perfectly with a well-made iced coffee. The café is also known for its colourful ube lattes, pandan frappes, and traditional halo-halo.

The friendly and generous staff run this business with pride. They create a cosy, community-centred atmosphere for all visitors. The team is always happy to offer recommendations to customers trying Filipino desserts for the first time. Whether you want to enjoy a dine-in sweet treat or grab takeaway pastries, this bakery offers an authentic experience. You can find them open daily from 9:30 am to 7:30 pm.

name: Rolling Express
description:
Rolling Express is a highly regarded cargo and sea freight forwarding service specialising in transporting door-to-door balikbayan boxes from Brisbane and the Gold Coast to all destinations in the Philippines. Operating since 2015, this professional service makes it simple for the local Filipino-Australian community to send packages, household items, and gifts home to their loved ones.

Local clients value the team’s helpfulness, reliability, and excellent communication. They keep customers informed with regular shipping progress notifications, providing great peace of mind. The service is known for delivering boxes safely and in good condition, even to regional provinces like Cebu and Davao del Sur, typically within a four-to-ten-week estimate from departure.

They ship containers containing personal effects, food, clothing, furniture, and bulky tools. Rolling Express handles everything from scheduled box pick-ups across Brisbane and the Gold Coast to container loading, shipping logistics, and customs clearance in Manila. The business is fully accredited as a sea freight forwarder by the Philippines Bureau of Customs. If you want a trustworthy forwarder to connect with your family back home, Rolling Express offers a very dependable and professional door-to-door solution.

name: Cuisina By Nena
description:
Experience the exceptional warmth of authentic Filipino hospitality at Cuisina By Nena, a beloved family-owned hidden gem in Burpengary, Queensland. Bringing a vibrant Filipino-Spanish fusion culinary experience to the local community, this cozy restaurant is celebrated for serving mouthwatering, home-style comfort food cooked with heart and love. The kitchen is led by Chef Joy, whose bubbly, incredibly hospitable service and dedication to her craft make every diner feel like part of the family, whether she is crafting custom off-the-menu creations or accommodating specific dietary needs like vegetarian and gluten-free diets. 

The menu boasts a stellar lineup of traditional favorites and modern creations, including fall-off-the-bone tender BBQ ribs, flame-grilled Chicken Inasal, crispy Pork Liempo, birria tacos, chorizo spring rolls, and savory Dinuguan served with fluffy puto. Make sure to save room for their highly praised, silky-smooth homemade Leche Flan. Embodying the rich spirit of Grandma Nena’s recipes elevated for today, Cuisina By Nena offers a wonderful dining experience with a welcoming, homely vibe that feels just like dining at Mama's house. Dine-in, takeaway, and delivery via Uber Eats are available, ensuring Burpengary locals can always satisfy their cravings for authentic Filipino comfort food.

name: Sabayan - Filipino Creative and Cultural Collective
description:
Sabayan is a vibrant Filipino creative and cultural collective based in Brisbane. Serving as a welcoming social and creative hub, the group focuses particularly on helping Filipino-Australian youth connect with their rich heritage and cultural customs. Through multidisciplinary arts and cultural programming, the collective encourages members to develop pride in their identity and explore their roots.

Local participants appreciate the wide range of hands-on educational workshops and community activities. These include playing traditional street games, learning the pre-colonial Baybayin script, and producing traditional wear. In addition to these showcases, Sabayan organises film screenings, panel discussions, and food festivals. These events share Filipino culture with the wider Brisbane public.

The collective also provides professional development opportunities like mentoring and coaching. They offer well-being support tailored for local artists and creative professionals. Operating in the Central ward of Brisbane, the collective works to challenge stereotypes. They aim to foster a strong sense of community belonging. The group showcases the unique talents and history of the Filipino diaspora. They provide a positive, supportive space where people can learn, express themselves, and grow together.

name: Sabor Cafe & Grill
description:
Sabor Cafe & Grill (also known as Kasina by Sabor) is a stunning, plant-filled hidden gem of a Filipino café, tucked away between industrial buildings on Station Road in Yeerongpilly, close to the local train station. Welcoming and full of heart, the cafe features a cosy interior with book nooks, a child-friendly outdoor play area, a leafy courtyard, and a wrap-around verandah.

Locals and visitors praise the exceptionally friendly service and generous portions of authentic Filipino-inspired dishes. The menu specialises in beautifully presented, simmered stews such as rich Caldereta, savoury Menudo, and Bicol Express with a sophisticated balance of heat and coconut cream. Other crowd favourites include crispy pork belly kare-kare, sisig, fried-egg-topped pork tocino, and chicken adobo.

To conclude a meal, guests can indulge in their famous banana bread, cassava cake, or ube desserts paired with a refreshing Matcha Ube Latte. With its relaxed neighbourhood spirit, Sabor Cafe & Grill is the perfect destination for both curious newcomers and discerning diners seeking to satisfy their cravings or start their day in a chilled, relaxed atmosphere.

name: Sama-Sama Connect (Impact Church)
description:
Sama-Sama Connect is a welcoming Filipino-Australian Christian fellowship and connect group hosted by Impact Church in Slacks Creek, Brisbane. Meeting every Tuesday evening at 7:00 PM, the group focuses on building strong community bonds, exploring faith, and sharing food in a supportive environment. The gatherings are hosted by Pastor Mari-shell, providing a comfortable space for individuals to connect and grow together.

As a ministry of Impact Church, attendees can also access a wider range of church services and activities. The church is highly regarded for its inspiring sermons, dedicated pastoral team, and family-friendly atmosphere. It features a state-of-the-art auditorium, an active young adults ministry, and children's programmes.

Locals particularly appreciate the church's community cafe, which operates during the week and serves great coffee. Additionally, the church supports the community through a food outlet that provides cheap groceries on specific days. This fellowship group offers a fantastic way for the Filipino community in Brisbane to find a place to belong, make new friends, and share in faith-focused conversations.

name: Samahang Pinoy sa Griffith
description:
Primarily based at the Nathan campus, this vibrant cultural club specialises in fostering camaraderie among Filipino students. The group welcomes members of all nationalities, assisting international students in integrating into the Australian community. Committee members also provide valuable support for student well-being during their university studies.

To celebrate and share the rich heritage of the Philippines, the organisation plans diverse events throughout the trimester. Members regularly gather for social coffee catchups, karaoke sessions, games nights, and traditional boodle fight feasts. The committee also maintains active communication channels like WhatsApp to coordinate study sessions, sports, and casual catchups. These activities help promote Filipino history, values, and culture on campus.

The club's strong engagement and contribution to student life earned it the 'New Club of the Year' title at the Griffith University Leadership Awards in 2023. Students looking to connect with the local community can register for membership online through the university's Campus Groups portal.

name: Shop NiKulas - Ipswich Filipino Store
description:
Shop NiKulas is a family-operated Filipino grocery store situated at 9 Broxburn Circuit in Spring Mountain, Queensland. Serving the local Ipswich region and surrounding suburbs, this convenient neighbourhood shop specialised in providing a comprehensive selection of authentic Filipino groceries, pantry staples, and beloved snacks. Customers can browse a well-stocked range of ingredients, including popular frozen products like Tender Juicy hotdogs, to prepare traditional meals at home.

The business operates from a local family garage, offering a welcoming atmosphere where the community can easily access a taste of the Philippines. Local shoppers frequently praise the extremely friendly service and appreciate having a dedicated Pinoy shop close to home. The store caters to different schedules by offering in-store shopping, local pickup, and delivery options across Australia.

To accommodate busy lifestyles, Shop NiKulas features flexible trading hours. The store opens in the evenings from 6:00 pm to 10:00 pm from Monday to Wednesday. For daytime shopping, it opens from 10:00 am to 10:00 pm on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday, and from 11:00 am to 10:00 pm on Sunday. This local gem makes it easy for the community to enjoy their favourite Filipino flavours.

name: St Flannan's Catholic Church
description:
St Flannan's Catholic Church, located in Zillmere in northern Brisbane, serves as a warm and welcoming place of worship for the local community, including a vibrant Filipino-Australian parish. Positioned on Handford Road, the church is known for its peaceful and solemn mass environment despite the busy road outside. Parishioners appreciate the hard-working priest, Father Rafael, and the friendly, helpful volunteers who make everyone feel welcome.

The church is well-equipped with modern technology, featuring screens hanging from the ceiling to help follow the liturgy and special services. The parish offers regular weekday masses from Monday to Friday, multiple Sunday services, and reconciliation on Saturday afternoons. On the second Sunday of the month, the parish hosts a dedicated Filipino Mass beginning with the Rosary at 11:30 AM, followed by Holy Mass at 12:00 PM, and a popular community fellowship lunch.

Beyond regular services, the parish celebrates traditional Filipino religious observances such as Simbang Gabi and Salubong, fostering a strong sense of cultural preservation. The community frequently organises social gatherings, offering tea, coffee, and light meals after Sunday services. St Flannan's Catholic Church continues to be a central spiritual and cultural hub in Zillmere, bringing together multicultural parishioners in a supportive environment.

name: St Flannan's Catholic Parish
description:
St Flannan's Catholic Parish is a warm and welcoming place of worship located on Handford Road in Zillmere, Brisbane. The parish is known for its active, multicultural congregation and has built strong connections with the local Filipino-Australian community.

Visitors and regular churchgoers frequently praise the friendly parish priest and helpful volunteers who make everyone feel at home. Although situated in a busy area, the low-set church provides a peaceful and solemn space for prayer and meditation. Inside, the modern design features screens hanging from the ceiling to help the congregation follow along with services.

The parish holds weekday Rosary and Mass from Monday to Friday, as well as Saturday Reconciliation and Vigil Mass. Sunday services are well-attended, featuring a dedicated Youth Mass on the third Sunday of the month. The church community is highly social, often organising tea, coffee, and light meals after services to encourage fellowship. St Flannan's is a wonderful spiritual home for anyone looking to connect with a caring, diverse local community in northern Brisbane.

name: St James Catholic Parish - Coorparoo
description:
St James Catholic Parish in Coorparoo is a welcoming and active multicultural church community within the Archdiocese of Brisbane. Run by the Augustinians, the parish is known for its warm hospitality and strong links to the local Filipino-Australian community. Parishioners are highly involved in community work, and the church holds a special place in the hearts of many families for its thoughtful baptism, Easter, and regular mass services.

Visitors and regulars appreciate the approachability of the clergy, particularly the supportive parish priests. The parish features a fantastic choir that enhances the liturgy, and the community frequently hosts morning tea gatherings run by local youth. A dedicated youth group also organises prayer sessions every Friday evening from 7:30 pm to 9:00 pm, except on the first Friday of the month. Regular weekend masses are held on Saturdays at 8:00 am and a 6:00 pm Vigil, and on Sundays at 7:00 am, 9:00 am, and 6:00 pm. Reconciliation is available on Saturdays from 4:30 pm to 5:30 pm. Weekday masses are scheduled at 8:00 am from Monday to Wednesday, and at 9:00 am on Fridays.

The parish is particularly celebrated for hosting the traditional Filipino Simbang Gabi Christmas novena masses from 16 to 24 December, followed by community fellowship. The church also works closely with the Australian Filipino Augustinian Solidarity (AFAS) group to support charity and development initiatives in both Australia and the Philippines.

name: St Joseph's & St Anthony's Catholic Church
description:
Located in Bracken Ridge, Brisbane, St Joseph's & St Anthony's Catholic Church is a vibrant and welcoming parish serving the local Filipino-Australian community. The church is renowned for its modern, sustainable architecture and stunning mountain views at the altar. Parishioners frequently praise the friendly congregation and the warm, genuine leadership of Father Bryan Pipins, whose enthusiastic and engaging homilies resonate deeply with the community.

The parish offers a variety of services, including regular weekday and weekend masses. Families appreciate the child-friendly facilities, such as a dedicated toy corner for toddlers and babies during service. A central highlight of the parish's spiritual life is its music ministry, featuring a highly praised choir accompanied by a piano and guitar ensemble. For the Filipino community, the church is a key cultural hub, hosting traditional Simbang Gabi preparations with carols, rosaries, and community fellowship before Christmas. Additionally, the parish is active in supporting youth and families through a well-organised volunteer-led sacramental program.

name: St Mark’s Catholic Parish
description:
St Mark's Catholic Parish in Inala is a welcoming, multicultural faith community located in Brisbane. The parish is known for its strong community spirit and rich cultural diversity, bringing together families from various backgrounds. Visitors and parishioners appreciate the warm hospitality and the peaceful, prayerful atmosphere of the church. The community is administered by the Divine Word Missionaries, and the pastoral team is highly regarded for their dedicated service, supportive presence, and willingness to assist families during significant life events.

As a vibrant centre of multicultural worship, the parish holds regular masses in English and Vietnamese throughout the week. It has a long-standing and active connection with the Filipino-Australian community. The parish proudly hosts the local Sto. Niño Devotees and organises the annual Sinulog Festival in January. This major celebration features traditional dances, food, and religious processions that bring the community together. In addition, the parish supports groups such as Couples for Christ, Youth for Christ, and various cultural choirs. St Mark's is a peaceful place of worship that celebrates diversity and offers a strong sense of belonging for everyone in the Inala area.

name: Sto Niño Devotees - St Mark's Inala
description:
The Sto Niño Devotees is a warm and active Filipino Catholic devotional group based at St Mark’s Catholic Parish in Inala, Brisbane. Administered by the Divine Word Missionaries, the parish is highly valued by its diverse congregation for its peaceful atmosphere and strong sense of community. The devotees group plays a central role in organising and preserving Filipino cultural and religious traditions within the local area.

Their most significant event is the annual Feast of Sto. Niño (Holy Child Jesus) and Sinulog celebration, which takes place every January. This vibrant festival draws many families together for a special Mass and religious procession, where members bring their Sto Niño statues to be blessed. The celebration also features a lively Sinulog parade with traditional music and dancing, followed by a shared community feast. Attendees enjoy Filipino culinary favourites such as lechon, alongside cultural performances and singing.

Led by dedicated community members like Aileen Richter, the group welcomes everyone who wishes to participate in these rich traditions. The parish centre at 96 Lilac Street serves as a welcoming gathering space for both worship and cultural fellowship throughout the year.

name: Story Behind The Coffee - Acacia Ridge
description:
Story Behind The Coffee is a welcoming, Filipino-owned specialty cafe located in Acacia Ridge, Brisbane. Founded by Danilo (Dani) Jovero Jr. and his wife Yumi, the cafe serves as a vibrant spot for coffee lovers and the local community. The name of the business highlights how a shared love of coffee first brought the couple together.

Locals highly recommend the cafe for its excellent specialty coffee, which is sourced ethically and sustainably from growers in the Philippines. Customers also love the friendly, fast service and the spacious parking area. In addition to premium brews, the cafe offers a delicious selection of authentic Filipino treats, including ube pastries, pandesal, and refreshing halo-halo. The cafe also sells specialty coffee beans, manual brewing equipment, and barista tools for home brewing.

Whether you are looking for a quick morning takeaway or a cosy spot to relax, this cafe is a wonderful local gem. It is a great place to enjoy a high-quality cup of coffee while connecting with friends in a warm and inviting atmosphere.

name: The Feast North Brisbane
description:
The Feast North Brisbane is a warm and inviting Catholic prayer gathering and community fellowship hosted in Aspley. Organised by the Light of Jesus Family Queensland, a movement founded in the Philippines by lay preacher Bo Sanchez, the ministry serves the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane's northern suburbs. Gatherings are held on the second Saturday of each month from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM at Our Lady and St Dympna's Parish.

Members of the congregation describe the parish as a peaceful and serene place of worship that offers a welcoming home for the soul. The community is celebrated for its hospitable, multicultural atmosphere, fostering deep spiritual growth and friendly connections. These monthly gatherings feature lively worship, uplifting preaching, and fellowship, providing a supportive space for families.

The venue itself is highly convenient for attendees, offering ample parking spaces and a well-equipped hall for community events and celebrations. Whether you are looking to deepen your faith or connect with fellow members of the Filipino-Australian community, this fellowship provides a vibrant, grace-filled environment where everyone is welcome.

name: The Feast South Brisbane
description:
The Feast South Brisbane is a warm and welcoming Catholic charismatic prayer gathering that serves the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane. Organised by the Light of Jesus Family (LOJ Family)—a global community founded by Bo Sanchez—this monthly fellowship provides a vibrant space for spiritual growth and connection. The gathering takes place on the fourth Saturday of every month, from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM, in the Avocado Room at Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic Church in Sunnybank.

Attendees can expect a lively and uplifting experience featuring heartfelt praise and worship, inspirational talks, scripture studies, and sharing sessions. The community focuses on helping members grow in their faith, build strong friendships, and find support among fellow believers. After the spiritual programme, participants gather for a light fellowship where they can share stories and strengthen community bonds.

Whether you are looking to deepen your relationship with God, connect with other Filipino families in Queensland, or simply enjoy a positive and encouraging environment, The Feast South Brisbane offers a supportive home. It is a wonderful opportunity to experience the warmth of Filipino hospitality and faith in a friendly local setting.

name: The Filipino Shop
description:
The Filipino Shop is a popular grocery store and casual eatery located in Southport's Chinatown, conveniently situated just opposite Australia Fair on the Gold Coast. Serving as a central hub for the local Filipino community, the venue combines retail shopping with warm, home-style dining and handy financial remittance services like MoneyGram and Western Union.

Under friendly new management, the shop welcomes visitors with helpful service and a fee-free card payment policy. On the grocery side, the shelves are well-stocked with a diverse range of imported snacks, noodles, traditional sauces, spices, and frozen ingredients. The adjoining kitchen specialises in hot Filipino dishes, featuring tender Cebu-style lechon, pork adobo, dinuguan, and sizzling sisig set meals. Customers particularly favour their freshly made spring rolls and refreshing halo-halo, which is widely celebrated for its balanced flavours and textures. With a casual and welcoming atmosphere, it is a fantastic spot to enjoy a relaxed lunch or stock up on pantry favourites.

name: The Kabayan Kusina
description:
The Kabayan Kusina was a beloved local takeaway restaurant located inside the Triple Cee Shopping Centre on Kingston Road in Logan Central, Queensland. Specialising in authentic and traditional Filipino cuisine, this eatery became a local favourite for its home-cooked meals that tasted just like home.

Customers highly praised the venue for its generous takeaway portions and fairly priced menu. The kitchen cooked all dishes fresh on-site, ensuring a high-quality dining experience. Popular options included their rotating weekend specials, featuring classic dishes such as gata ng langka (jackfruit in coconut milk), savoury pork picadillo, and sweet and sour fish fillet. The menu was designed to make traditional Filipino comfort food accessible to both the local Filipino-Australian community and curious food lovers in the Logan City area.

Although the restaurant was initially known for its friendly service and convenient location, the business has now permanently closed at this Kingston Road venue. Former patrons looking for similar authentic Filipino flavours are encouraged to visit Pinoy Delights Sunnybank, where they can enjoy dine-in, takeaway, and pre-orders.

name: Tita's Canteen
description:
Tita's Canteen is a welcoming Filipino eatery located on Level E of the Uptown Shopping Centre in the heart of Brisbane City. Established by former superyacht chef Ginger Mendoza Andersen, this hidden gem specialises in homestyle comfort food and traditional merienda snacks that make visitors feel right at home.

Local diners love the warm hospitality, frequently praising the exceptionally friendly service provided by JP and the rest of the team. The canteen serves up authentic Filipino flavours, with popular all-day breakfast silog meals including juicy longganisa, tocino, and tapsilog served alongside garlic rice and egg. Visitors also rave about the rich, homemade steam dumplings, comforting pork adobo, and the crispy chicken skin and chicharon. For a sweet treat, the canteen offers customisable halo-halo, ube soft serve, and refreshing sago't gulaman.

Whether you are stopping by for a quick lunch after church or picking up a takeaway snack on your daily commute, Tita's Canteen provides a delicious taste of the Philippines in a convenient location. It is open daily, making it a favourite spot for both the Filipino community and curious locals to enjoy a satisfying meal.

name: Tito Rolly's Filipino Store
description:
Tito Rolly's Filipino Store is a welcoming neighbourhood grocery shop located in Fitzgibbon, Brisbane. Stepping inside feels reminiscent of visiting a classic sari-sari or convenience store in the Philippines, offering a comforting slice of home for the local community. The shop features a wide variety of imported grocery brands, pantry staples, authentic Pinoy snacks, and fresh ingredients to satisfy traditional cravings.

Beyond the retail shelves, customers highly appreciate the exceptional hospitality and friendly service provided by Tito Rolly and his team. Visitors often highlight the warm, helpful environment, making it a favourite spot to chat and connect. In addition to packaged goods and popular desserts, the store offers a selection of delicious, ready-to-eat Filipino meals, including classic dishes like adobo and sinigang. Located at 184 Carselgrove Avenue, Tito Rolly's Filipino Store has established itself as a cherished hub for the Filipino-Australian community in the northern suburbs of Brisbane, providing high-quality products at reasonable prices.

name: Tito Rolly's Filipino Store - Fitzgibbon
description:
Tito Rolly's Filipino Store in Fitzgibbon is a welcoming, family-run grocery shop that brings the taste and feel of the Philippines to Brisbane. The store is well known for its friendly, helpful service, with owner Rolly and the staff making everyone feel like part of the family. Walking into the shop feels like stepping into a traditional Filipino convenience store, filled with familiar comforts and nostalgic items.

The shelves are fully stocked with a wide variety of authentic Pinoy groceries, pantry essentials, ingredients, snacks, and sweets. In addition to packaged goods, customers can find popular desserts and a selection of delicious, freshly prepared dishes like adobo and sinigang. Everything is offered at reasonable prices, making it a favourite spot for the local community to satisfy their cravings.

Conveniently located on Carselgrove Avenue in Fitzgibbon, Tito Rolly's is a great place for the Filipino-Australian community and anyone looking to explore authentic Filipino flavours. Whether you are shopping for weekly ingredients or looking for a comforting hot meal, this store offers a warm, helpful, and satisfying experience.

name: Tony's Filipino Bread
description:
Tony's Filipino Bread is a popular local bakery located in the East Caboolture Village in Caboolture, Queensland. The bakery specialises in authentic Filipino baked goods, pastries, and breads. It serves as a go-to spot for the local Filipino-Australian community.

Visitors frequently praise the friendly, lovely, and helpful staff who welcome everyone warmly. Among their most popular items are the freshly baked pan de sal and pan de sal asado, which customers describe as tasty and highly authentic. The bakery is also known for serving delicious sausage rolls and a variety of sweet treats. For special celebrations, the team can prepare custom cakes, including traditional ube birthday cakes.

Operating under new management, the bakery offers high-quality products at very reasonable and budget-friendly prices. While the venue is a hidden gem for takeaway bakery items, they also supply their fresh breads to various Asian and Filipino grocery stores across the wider Brisbane metropolitan area. It is open daily from Monday to Saturday, providing fresh treats for the community.

name: Turbot Street Medical Centre (Dr Karin Mape)
description:
Turbot Street Medical Centre is a family-oriented general practice located in Spring Hill, Brisbane. Conveniently situated on Level 1 of the Spring Hill Marketplace Shopping Centre, the clinic specialises in delivering high-quality healthcare services to the local community. The practice is open 365 days a year, providing reliable access to medical professionals, including weekend and public holiday sessions.

Patients frequently praise the clinic's welcoming atmosphere, noting that both the reception team and nursing staff are friendly and communicative. The practitioners are highly regarded for their caring approach, taking the time to listen, explain medical matters clearly, and follow up thoroughly on patient care. The diverse team of general practitioners includes Dr Karin Mape, who is fluent in both English and Filipino (Tagalog), which is a valuable support for Filipino-Australian families seeking culturally accessible care.

The clinic offers comprehensive health services, such as women's health, adult medicine, chronic disease management, and preventive care. With convenient online booking, seamless payment processes, and same-day appointment options, Turbot Street Medical Centre ensures a positive, supportive experience for all patients.

name: UGH Coffee
description:
UGH Coffee is a welcoming Filipino-Australian fusion café and pantry located on Logan Road in Underwood, Queensland. It has grown from a humble Filipino pantry into a cozy local spot where community members can relax and unwind.

The café specialises in unique Filipino-inspired specialty drinks and classic coffee. Customers highly recommend the signature iced ube espresso, ube matcha lattes, and the banana caramel latte with cold foam. Alongside the drink menu, the café serves delicious savoury and sweet treats. These include cheesy Philly toasties, ham and cheese croissants, and traditional Filipino snacks like empanadas, lumpia, and longganisa buns. At the back, a grocery section stocks authentic Filipino cooking ingredients and frozen meals like adobo and sisig.

Visitors appreciate the bright, clean space and the friendly, laidback service from the staff. Whether you drop in for a quick takeaway or stay to relax in the lounge, UGH Coffee offers a warm taste of Filipino hospitality.

name: UQ Filipino-Australian Student Association
description:
The Association of Filipinos and Filipino-Australians at the University of Queensland, commonly known as FilOz UQ, is a vibrant, student-led cultural and social organisation affiliated with the University of Queensland Union (UQU). The society serves as a welcoming support network and a true home away from home for Filipino students, helping them settle into university life in Brisbane while fostering intergenerational connections.

FilOz UQ actively celebrates and promotes Philippine heritage, language, and culture on campus. Members and the broader student body can participate in a variety of engaging activities, including cultural celebrations, sports events, networking nights, and social gatherings. The group also organises community initiatives, such as charity donation drives, and partners with local venues like Upbeat Cafe to offer exclusive benefits to its members.

Whether you are an international student seeking a familiar community, a local student of Filipino descent wanting to connect with your heritage, or simply interested in experiencing Filipino culture, FilOz UQ offers a warm, inclusive space for everyone. You can easily connect with the committee and join the society through their active Facebook and Instagram profiles.

name: Ube Cakes and Bakes
description:
Ube Cakes and Bakes is a home-based specialty bakery located in Mango Hill, Queensland. Founded by passionate home baker Lizzette Pena, the bakery was born out of a desire to share authentic Filipino flavours and heritage with her family and the wider community. Specialising in custom-made cakes and cupcakes, they prepare every order fresh to help make local celebrations truly memorable.

The menu highlights traditional Filipino favourites, including moist and fluffy classic ube cream cake, ube macapuno, rich yema cake, and buko pandan cake. Other popular choices include their signature sans rival cake, mocha cake, and seasonal mango cake, alongside classic chocolate, carrot, and strawberry options. For smaller gatherings, they offer mini cupcake versions of most cake flavours, plus traditional mamon sponge cakes. All creations are baked fresh from Lizzette's home kitchen, requiring pre-booking to ensure quality. While the business previously offered its custom creations to locals across Mango Hill and surrounding suburbs, this business has permanently closed.

name: United Pentecostal Church City of Moreton Bay
description: United Pentecostal Church City of Moreton Bay is a welcoming Christian congregation that serves the Filipino-Australian community in Caboolture and the wider Moreton Bay region. Formerly known as the United Pentecostal Church of Caboolture, the church is affiliated with the United Pentecostal Church of Australia (UPCA). Under the leadership of Pastors Reverend and Mrs Jan Rex Suaybaguio, the congregation provides a supportive environment for worship, prayer, and spiritual connection. The church holds its weekly Sunday services and Children's Church at 9:30 am, meeting at the Caboolture Combined Services Hall located at 21 Hayes Street. This local assembly focuses on encouraging spiritual growth and offering guidance to families in the area. Members appreciate the friendly atmosphere and the active involvement of the pastoral team, who facilitate regular fellowship and community outreach programs. Whether you are visiting for the first time or looking for a regular place to worship in the Moreton Bay area, the church welcomes everyone to join their Sunday morning activities.

name: Wil's Resto
description:
Wil's Resto is a relaxed and friendly Filipino restaurant located in the bustling Market Square of Sunnybank, Brisbane. It specialises in serving authentic, comfort-filled Filipino dishes that offer excellent value and generous portion sizes. Local diners highly recommend their signature loaded sizzling pork sisig, which arrives crackling on a hot plate topped with chicharon and an egg. Other favourites include the exceptionally crispy pata with crunchy skin, comforting beef pares with bone marrow, boneless milkfish (bangus) served with calamansi sauce, and the popular BBQ platters. For dessert, the traditional halo-halo is a standout favourite that consistently hits the spot, alongside cassava cake and refreshing house sodas.

The casual, indoor dining space features a welcoming atmosphere with a unique food map decoration, making it a popular hub for local families. While the lively space can get a bit loud and echoey when busy, the friendly staff ensure quick service and a warm dining experience. Self-serve water is conveniently located near the counter next to the neon sign. Whether you are popping in for a quick lunch combo or sharing a hearty dinner with family, Wil's Resto delivers a satisfying taste of the Philippines right in Sunnybank.

name: Winston's Bakeshop and Resto
description:
Winston's Bakeshop and Resto is a welcoming Filipino restaurant and bakery situated in Logan Central. This popular local dining spot combines a hot kitchen menu with a traditional bakery, offering dine-in and takeaway options for breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

Customers praise the fresh, soft baked goods, highlighting the Spanish bread, binangkal, cheese rolls, bread pudding, and sweet donuts as standout choices. The kitchen team specialises in classic Filipino main dishes, serving flavour-packed favourites such as sizzling pork, chicken sisig, dinuguan, and pinakbet. Their traditional beef tongue dish, lengua, is a popular off-menu specialty. Guests also enjoy the comforting chicken tinola broth and hearty tapsilog breakfast, followed by a refreshing halo-halo dessert or a freshly brewed barista coffee.

The restaurant is known for its generous portions, friendly staff, and a warm atmosphere that makes visitors feel like family. Located at Shop 1/84 Wembley Road, the venue offers a casual dining experience with ample free parking options in the adjacent lot. It is a perfect destination for both the local Filipino community and those trying the cuisine for the first time.

name: World2Australia Migration Solutions
description:
World2Australia Migration Solutions is a professional migration and education consultancy based in the Brisbane Technology Park at Eight Mile Plains. Led by registered migration agent Gloria Collins (MARN 1387135) and a licensed New Zealand immigration adviser, the agency specialised in visa applications for Australia and New Zealand.

Clients highly recommend the team for their clear guidance, thorough preparation, and personalised approach. The agency regularly assists applicants with graduate visas (subclass 485), subsequent entrant visas, student visas, and permanent residency applications (including subclasses 489 and 887). Reviewers often praise staff members Andy, Norris, and Chantelle for their professional communication and supportive nature, especially when handling complex visa cases.

The office is open Monday to Friday from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, and on Saturdays from 9:00 am to 2:00 pm. It is closed on Sundays. They provide services tailored to the Filipino-Australian community in Brisbane, ensuring migration pathways are handled with care and efficiency.

name: Yellow Yolks Silvanas
description: Yellow Yolks Silvanas is a licensed home-based bakery in Browns Plains, Queensland, specialising in handcrafting traditional Filipino frozen desserts for the Brisbane community. This boutique business is celebrated for its premium silvanas—crispy cashew-meringue cookies filled with rich buttercream—available in vanilla, chocolate, and assorted party boxes. Customers love that these treats have a perfectly balanced sweetness and a soft, chewy texture that brings back fond memories of the Philippines. In addition to their signature silvanas, they bake traditional Sans Rival cakes, cheesy chiffon cakes, and beautifully designed custom cupcakes for special events. The business maintains a very high standard of hygiene, proudly holding a four-star Eat Safe Logan rating from the Logan City Council. The owner is highly regarded for providing friendly, quick, and responsive service, making the entire ordering and pick-up process seamless. Customers can place their orders online through their website for scheduled pick-up by appointment at their Browns Plains location.

name: Yes Please Filipino Restaurant Dine in & Takeaway
description:
Yes Please Filipino Restaurant Dine in & Takeaway was a popular casual dining spot located on Wellington Road in East Brisbane, Queensland. It specialised in serving authentic Filipino cuisine and classic comfort dishes in a warm, welcoming environment.

The restaurant was highly regarded by the local Filipino community and adventurous food lovers alike. Regular customers frequently praised the restaurant's signature crispy pata, sizzling pork sisig, and savoury vinegar pork stew, which were often accompanied by garlic rice. Other popular menu options included deep-fried quail egg skewers, vermicelli, and classic Filipino barbecue skewers. Diners particularly appreciated the generous portions, with the massive crispy pork knuckle often described as a hearty meal for two people. Most mains were reasonably priced between ten and twenty dollars, offering excellent value for money.

The dining space was spacious and featured side tables equipped with games to keep customers entertained. Many guests highlighted the clean kitchen, which remained visible and spotless even during busy rush-hour dinners. Although the counter service and locating the venue could occasionally be challenging, the abundant parking space in front of the venue made it a convenient spot to visit.

This business has permanently closed.

name: ZOE Event Hire
description:
ZOE Event Hire is a boutique event styling business based in Brisbane, Queensland, specialising in custom balloon garlands and elegant faux floral designs. Operated by Nina Mendoza, a passionate stylist with professional experience dating back to 2012, this business serves clients within 100 kilometres of Greenbank, covering Ipswich, Logan, and the Gold Coast.

Clients consistently praise the business for its personalised approach, excellent communication, and creative styling. Whether organising a first birthday party, a baby shower, a baptism, or a milestone thirtieth birthday, clients love how Nina works closely with them to bring their visual themes to life. The high-quality organic balloon arches and custom welcome signs are popular features that elevate any celebration.

The team handles the entire process, including prop hire such as arches, backdrops, and cake stands. For enquiries and bookings, you can reach out directly via email at hello@zoeeventhire.com.au. The business is fully insured and operates from Monday to Friday between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM, remaining closed on weekends.

name: Zenith Lounge Barber & Cafe
description:
Zenith Lounge Barber & Cafe is an award-winning contemporary hybrid space in Tarragindi, Brisbane, that merges a professional barber studio with a speciality café. Founded by young entrepreneur Miguel David, this unique local venue is home to Lola's Coffee Bar downstairs, serving ceremonial-grade matcha, espresso, and Filipino-inspired treats like ube, pandan, and longganisa rolls. Upstairs, the modern grooming studio offers tailored haircuts, fades, and precise scissor cuts.

The local community highly recommends this spot for its energetic, skilled team of barbers, including Visal, Vinzent, Cooper, Elijah, and Rori. Customers frequently praise their technical expertise, patience with children, and meticulous attention to detail. More than just a place for a haircut, the studio is celebrated for its positive vibes, positive hospitality, and welcoming atmosphere.

Conveniently located on Ekibin Road East, this Tarragindi destination is open from Tuesday to Sunday, with extended hours on Friday evenings. It is a popular community hub where clients can enjoy premium grooming alongside delicious coffee and desserts.

name: Zenith Lounge Barber & Cafe - Kelvin Grove
description:
Zenith Lounge Barber & Cafe is a unique contemporary hybrid space located in Kelvin Grove, Brisbane, combining a modern barber studio with a downstairs café known as Lola's Coffee Bar. This welcoming venue is situated within the Zest Apartments on Musk Avenue, serving as a vibrant community hub. Upstairs, the award-winning barber studio specialises in tailored haircuts, premium grooming, scissor cuts, and precise skin fades. Downstairs, the café complements the experience by serving ceremonial-grade matcha, specialty coffee, and a delicious selection of Filipino-inspired treats, including ube, pandan, and longganisa rolls.

Local clients highly praise the barbers, particularly Jericho, Visal, and owner Miguel, for their passion, knowledge, and friendly conversation. Visitors appreciate the chill and comfortable lounge area, where they can play the PlayStation 5 while waiting. The service feels premium from start to finish, complete with a complimentary drink and a relaxing hot towel treatment at the end of each session. The staff are renowned for their attentive listening skills and dedication to understanding each client's preferences. Whether you are looking for a fresh haircut or a peaceful spot to enjoy a matcha latte, this Kelvin Grove local centre delivers a top-tier, personalised experience.

name: elise’spresso
description:
elise’spresso is a family-owned minimalist cafe and Filipino pantry located in Browns Plains, Queensland. Serving the local community, this welcoming venue combines a specialty coffee shop with a built-in Filipino grocery store.

Locals love the cosy atmosphere and friendly customer service. The cafe is particularly popular for its top-tier matcha beverages, including the highly rated pistachio iced matcha and traditional iced matcha. The extensive drinks menu also features specialty coffees, such as long black with ube foam and iced Spanish lattes.

For food, visitors can enjoy a variety of fresh sandwiches, toasties, bagels, and croissants. On weekends, the cafe serves popular Filipino silog breakfast meals. A wide selection of traditional Filipino sweets and pastries is available, including ube cream cheese ensaymada, egg pie, halo-halo, and ube flan cake.

In the back, the built-in pantry allows customers to browse imported Filipino grocery staples, such as hotdogs, biscuits, and pantry favourites. The cafe provides a comfortable space for friend meetups or special meetings, and table bookings can be made directly. Whether you are seeking a comforting cup of coffee or looking to stock up on Filipino ingredients, elise’spresso offers a delightful cultural and culinary experience.

name: J & M Filipino Cleaning Services
description:
J & M Filipino Cleaning Services is a family-owned and fully insured cleaning company based in Griffin, Queensland. Serving Brisbane and surrounding areas, they specialise in a wide range of residential, commercial, and deep cleaning solutions. Their core offerings include spring cleaning, bond or end-of-lease cleans, Airbnb preparation, window washing, and high-pressure cleaning. They also provide dry and steam carpet cleans, oven detailing, and indoor grout restoration.

The company is rooted in traditional Filipino values of diligence, integrity, and warm hospitality. Customers frequently praise the team, particularly operators Malou and Jaymee, for their reliability, promptness, and excellent communication throughout every project. Clients highlight their exceptional attention to detail, noting that they leave homes sparkling clean from top to bottom.

J & M Filipino Cleaning Services works closely with local real estate agencies, supporting tenants and property managers with professional bond cleaning. They are also trusted providers for domestic assistance, age care, and disability support organisations across the region. By combining eco-friendly cleaning practices with a dedication to personalised care, they ensure high-quality results that treat every client like family.
